<|endoftext|>Tissue plasminogen activation inhibitor is a new serine protease inhibitor derived from plasminogen.
We have cloned an endoglycosidase H (endo H) inhibitor, designated tissue plasminogen activation inhibitor, from plasminogen. Purified tissue plasminogen activation inhibitor is a single polypeptide chain with a molecular weight of 34 kD, consisting of 127 amino acid residues. Immunoblot analysis showed one major band to be tissue plasminogen activation inhibitor, although different isoforms are found in several species. Amino acid analysis indicated that tissue plasminogen activation inhibitor was a glycoprotein. A sequence homology search showed that tissue plasminogen activation inhibitor from a variety of species was very much like a new serine protease inhibitor called serpin.<|endoftext|>

<|endoftext|>[Genetic background of myopia].
Myopia is an important cause of vision loss and blindness, but factors determining the prevalence of myopia remain unknown. Most epidemiological studies on myopia were conducted in adults; however, few studies on genetics of myopia and their results are reported in adults. The aim of this study was to investigate the association of candidate genes with increased myopia in both familial and sporadic myopia using the microsomia database in Taiwan. By performing a case-control study, we analyzed 7,934 unrelated individuals from the microsomia database. Using the Haplothrombosis of genes in Taiwan (HOT) as a case-control matching instrument, we compared 7,934 myopic individuals and 6,145 nonmyopic individuals (control group) for polymorphisms in 13 candidate genes using the polymerase chain reaction-restriction fragment length polymorphism (PCR-RFLP) method. There were no polymorphisms identified in ABCA4 and SLC4A1. The allelic frequency in COL1A1, COL1A2, COL5A1, COL7A1 and COL11A1 was significantly different between the myopia group and control group, with p <

<|endoftext|>Q:

I was reading an article about the 'Rome II' Project and I encountered a sentence: "While [the project] ran three generations of R&D, they still had to develop a new chip, which they did in record time."

'Rome II' was a project that aimed to shrink everything to a microchip size, by using the technology in the 'Rome' project.
However, I got lost in the whole story
[the project] ran three generations of R&D, they still had to develop a new chip, which they did in record time. (see here for a link)
What is "record time"?

A:

While the original and follow-on projects ran to completion, they still had to develop a new chip--which they did in record time.
There are two parts to this sentence that I think are relevant:

the original and follow-on projects ran to completion, but

[the project] ran three generations of R&D, but the follow-on project ran only two generations.

record time is the technical term here, not a record time for the project.

<|endoftext|>We are excited to announce that we are now working on two new features in version 2.3.0 of Qbittorent:

Passthrough support for downloading encrypted files from torrent sites

Support for HTTP proxies

Passthrough

The torrent downloader in version 2.3 is now able to automatically download encrypted files even when the Qbittorent binary is loaded and started with --listing disabled.

This means you can now use the torrent client built into Qbittorent without having to go off to a website or open torrents manually. This is great news for Qbittorent 2.3.0 downloaders, too.

This feature is particularly useful for sites which block downloads and which have no external clients which can download .torrent files. If you use another torrent client, and would like to be able to open the .torrent files that the torrent client generated, Qbittorent is a good option to use.

The Qbittorent 2.3 binaries have also been modified as such that they work properly with Passthrough. This is already an excellent feature, but we hope we can make it even more usable by adding HTTP

<|endoftext|>In situ characterization of the interactions between amaranth and phytopathogenic fungi.
We tested the in situ interaction of a fungal pathogen fungal spherules with amaranth and found a significant correlation between disease incidence and fungal spherule number. These data suggest that a potential role for fungi in the etiology of canker of kiwifruit. In a set of tests designed to distinguish amaranth-phytopathogen interactions on the basis of host response, we found that a significant number of kiwifruit seedlings showed an inverse relationship between fungal spherule content and disease occurrence, indicating that the fungus interacted more frequently with fruit with lower amaranth seedling values than with fruit with higher amaranth seedling values.<|endoftext|>

<|endoftext|>Rational optimization of nanocomposite film properties as a function of nanostructure and morphology.
Polymer nanocomposites were recently proposed as a smart and renewable alternative for several applications, but the development of an optimized nanocomposite structure and a detailed characterization of their properties is very essential due to the significant role of morphology in the resulting material in both their processability as well as their physical and functional properties. In the present paper, we report on the nanostructured and morphology-controlled properties of poly(ε-caprolactone) in which poly(trimethylene carbonate) was employed in the nanocomposite. Two-dimensional (2D) homogeneous nanoscale network nanocomposites with different poly(trimethylene carbonate) content and morphologies were prepared and used to fabricate 3D nanocomposite structures. We investigated the effect of nanostructure and morphology on the nanocomposite properties and the effect of both on the nanocomposite properties by selecting the appropriate nanocomposite structures and morphologies. 2D random copolymer films with various nanoscale network morphology were prepared and 3D nanocomposite structures were fabricated by using the as-pre

<|endoftext|>Aminotransferase levels in neonates of diabetic mothers are comparable with those of control newborn infants.
The prevalence of hyperaminoacidemia in children of diabetic mothers is increased compared with non-diabetic mothers. To investigate the prevalence and type of histopathological alterations induced by hyperaminoacidemia in neonates of diabetic mothers. Thirty-two diabetic and 31 control newborn infants were included. The diagnosis of type 1 diabetes was considered the principal underlying cause. Anamnesis, clinical examination, abdominal ultrasound and abdominal/pelvic computed tomography were performed. Blood samples of 10 ml were obtained by peripheral venous sampling, and analyzed for alanine aminotransferase, total bilirubin and direct bilirubin levels. Histopathological findings were studied in all cases. The prevalence and type of the histopathological alterations were investigated and compared between groups. The serum levels of total bilirubin and direct bilirubin were similar in diabetic and control newborn infants. Histopathologically, no significant differences were observed between groups in liver, biliary cysts, pancreatic cysts, pancreatitis or glomerulonephritis. <|endoftext|>The effect of the P5 adenosine to adenosine ratio on cardiac electrical stability after rapid atrial pacing in humans.
The importance of the P50 adenosine to adenosine ratio, an index of mitochondrial energetic state, after ventricular pacing in humans has not been clearly understood. We assessed the effect of rapid atrial pacing on cardiac electrical instability in 16 patients without organic heart diseases. An incremental pacemaker was programmed to have a cycle length of 300 msec, with the maximum pulse width at the beginning of the P wave of approximately 120 msec during sinus rhythm, in patients with the P50/P90 adenosine ratio below or above the upper and lower limit of the normal range, respectively. In most P50/P90 ratios the heart rhythm was stable, while pacing in one-third of them atrial pacing was followed by sinus node dysfunction or complete heart block and tachyarrhythmias. A slow-fast ventricular pacing cycle length of 600 msec was not successful, unless P50/P90 and heart to heart beat interval ratio were under control (mean +/- standard deviation: 0.33 +/- 0.24 and 1.28 +/- 0.44, respectively, vs 0.92 +/- 0.55 and

<|endoftext|>The role of protein disulfide isomerase in cardiac development.
Protein disulfide isomerase (PDI) is a ubiquitous protein that catalyses the isomerization of disulfide bridges in secreted, membrane-bound, and soluble forms. It is involved in the regulation of cellular transport, protein folding, and protein maturation. The present review summarizes our current knowledge on the role of PDI in embryogenesis of heart. At least four PDI isoforms exist, namely, PDI-1, PDI-2, PDI-3, and PDI-4, and the latter two interact with the beta subunit of heterotrimeric G proteins. The present article will review the possible role of these isoforms in various stages of left-right determination of heart and their contribution to the development of heart.<|endoftext|>

<|endoftext|>The effect of vitamin D3 on growth and development of primary and secondary hyperparathyroidism.
The aim of this study was to examine the effect of vitamin D3 supplementation on the growth and development of patients with primary hyperparathyroidism (pHPT) and secondary hyperparathyroidism of osteomalacia (SHPTM). We used the case control study design. This study was approved by the Research Ethics Board at Istanbul University. We included in the study 48 patients with pHPT and 48 patients with SHPTM (pHPT, 17-70 kg; SHPTM, 70-120 kg) randomly assigned into two groups before starting the placebo protocol. All the included patients received vitamin D3 as the first medication and the control group on receiving the placebo for 1, 2, 3, 4 and 6 months. For evaluation the mean of growth rate of bone area (BA) within two height standard deviation and the height standard deviation for gestational age were used. Mean BA of vitamin D3 group and control group increased significantly and was not statistically different except in the 2- and 3-month assessments (P>0.05). In pHPT group, mean BA of vitamin D3 group was statistically increased at the end of 24

<|endoftext|>A man who attacked his neighbor at gunpoint, killing him, was arrested after police learned his wife was going to kill herself.

According to the arrest affidavit released Sunday, Anthony Wayne Liles, 55, admitted to officers that he had gone to the home of 34-year-old Justin Blanchard on Sunday and then fired his .45-caliber handgun while demanding that the man open the door.

At that time, Liles told the officers that he believed that if he pulled the trigger in his wife, she would die, officials said.

After Liles was arrested, officers found a note on his telephone stating that he would hurt himself if the person who wrote the note died.

Blanchard was found dead on the porch at the home and appeared to have been shot multiple times.

Police arrested Liles and found a loaded gun while he was carrying $200 and a note at the murder scene.

The note states Liles was distraught and wrote on a piece of paper: “If I pull the trigger, take my wife.”

An arrest record shows that Liles has previous arrests for burglary, burglary of an unattended vehicle and resisting, obstructing or delaying an officer.

<|endoftext|>[Experimental study on the biocompatibility of PEO-PPO-PEO block copolymer].
An experimental study was carried out for biocompatibility using the two types of the polyether block copolymer PEO-PPO-PEO (C(60-70) and C(80-90) wt%) and the pure styrene-type resin as control. The block copolymers were made from PEO and PP by atom transfer radical polymerization technique. These block copolymers were prepared using various amount of PEO (0% to 25% wt) and PPO(3% to 5% wt). These polymers were applied on bovine pericardia (3mm in diameter) after ultrasonic stirring for 5 minutes. In vitro tests such as hemolysis test, oxygen permeability test and cell attachment test were conducted using blood agar plate. In vivo tests were conducted using rabbit ear. In vitro results were almost similar, while the in vivo results of the PEO-PPO block copolymer showed slight hemolysis and strong cell attachment.<|endoftext|>

<|endoftext|>This proposal seeks three primary goals: 1) to design and test mathematical models for a system of multiple coupled systems of O-isoprenyltransferases (OPTs) that produce the essential lipids in both bacteria and animals. The proposed system is particularly interesting because it includes a number of important pathways for non-membrane-bound long-chain fatty acid (LCFA) synthesis that have never been considered together in the biological context. 2) to establish the functional relationship between different levels of the proposed system, as well as the functional relationship between the LCFA products and the cellular fatty acyl CoA pools, through experiments designed to measure the flux of different molecules through a number of pathways, and 3) to explore the mechanistic relationships between the levels of the proposed system and the system as a whole in developing mathematical models. Finally, we will combine mathematical modeling with experiments designed to measure rates of exchange of LCFA through a number of pathways. These experiments will make use of microsomes and intact bacteria from yeast and higher eukaryotes (human). We will also use the yeast cells to develop and validate quantitative models of the entire system, and to test the importance of certain enzymatic reactions. <|endoftext|>This is an archived article that was published on sltrib.com in 2013, and information in the article may be outdated. It is provided only for personal research purposes and may not be reprinted.

In a stunning reversal, a U.S. appeals court rules a group of Utah business owners who claimed they were victims of price discrimination were wrongly shut out of Utah state court, denying them a chance to find out whether an elected official and her husband discriminated against them for refusing to pay a higher tax rate on their sales tax collections.

In a 9-0 decision on Monday, a three-judge panel of the 10th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in Denver ruled the ruling against the owners of two Utah convenience stores was a mistake.

In dismissing the appeal in this case, Judge Mark C. Anderson of Salt Lake City said the owners’ "claim was dismissed on the merits for reasons the trial court specifically found and for reasons expressly included in our order denying plaintiffs’ motion for summary judgment."

The Supreme Court last year ruled that the state could not, via a referendum, allow businesses like the owners of the convenience stores to avoid collecting an excise tax by classifying their sales as part of their gross income.

<|endoftext|>The present invention relates to a semiconductor memory device, more particularly, to a semiconductor memory device, particularly, an SRAM, having a cell configuration with a memory array and a peripheral circuit.
Semiconductor memory devices such as static random access memories (hereinafter simply referred to as SRAMs) include a cell array for storing data, memory-initiating cells (hereinafter also simply referred to as memory cells) for controlling operations of the memory cells and input and output (hereinafter also simply referred to as input/output cells) for transferring input or output data provided by the cells. Each of the memory-initiating cells has access transistors for accessing predetermined memory cells in the cell and one of the access transistors is connected to a word line, and the other one of the access transistors is connected to a bit line. In a conventional semiconductor memory device such as an SRAM, some of the peripheral circuits including transistors to be controlled by the input/output cells are arranged on one chip in addition to the periphery of the cell array. For example, a CMOS circuit is arranged on the periphery of the cell array. An SRAM with such cell array and the peripheral circuit configuration is known as a

<|endoftext|>[Rescue therapy for the newborn infant with hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy].
The aim of this study was to evaluate the efficacy of inhaled nitrous oxide in the newborn infant with severe hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy. It had been confirmed that these patients can be treated successfully with inhaled nitrous oxide without significant adverse reactions. The authors present 3 cases of severe hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy of different etiologies treated with 60% inhaled nitrous oxide. The efficacy of nitrous oxide was also confirmed in the control group of 7 infants with severe hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy due to different causes. It is concluded that this method, being devoid of adverse effects, is the method of choice when hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy occurs in the newborn infant.<|endoftext|>

<|endoftext|>[Nasal and systemic allergic inflammatory disorders].
The authors report the case of a 23 year-old woman with a diagnosis of a fungal-allergic rhinoconjunctivitis, without any symptoms to asthma, urticaria or angioedema that worsened after several weeks. An allergic reaction to the fungus Aspergillus fumigatus was demonstrated by a complete skin test with the extract of the hyaluronic acid of the fungus A. fumigatus. The histological examination of the tissues obtained by the rhinoendoscopic and the nasal biopsy techniques showed the presence of allergic infiltrates of the nasal mucosa and, at the same time, a specific immunoglobulins in the plasma and a positive skin test to the A. fumigatus extract. The literature is assessed to find out, whether the rhinosinusitis found has any relationship with other diseases of the nasal mucosa and, consequently, the respiratory system.<|endoftext|>

<|endoftext|>A group of women have taken matters into their own hands when one of their fellow teachers suggested trying to catch their men sleeping on the premises because they “cuss a blue streak” every single morning. The class had an idea about the whole thing, which involved setting up an empty coffee table in the lunchroom. Students were told to place their empty mugs on top of it, and then the mugs stayed put for a week to see if anyone came by to drink out of it.

“If the men showed up, they would say the mug they put there was dirty. If they didn’t show up, they would say their mugs were clean. The mugs stay on the table for a week until the men arrive.” They went on to reveal the men’s names.

The present invention relates to an apparatus and method for encoding moving pictures and, more particularly, to an improved apparatus and method for efficiently and inexpensively encoding moving pictures.
2. Description of the Related Art
In order to record moving pictures on a recording medium so as to be reproduced by a reproducing apparatus such as a video cassette recorder (VCR) or a personal computer, it has been a common practice to encode moving pictures. In general, a time-division multiplexing system is used. In a time-division coding system, each pixel (picture element) is subjected to prediction encoding through interpolation or the like in units of a predetermined number of pixels along either a vertical or horizontal direction in a screen. A motion prediction system is also used which encodes moving pictures by generating, taking into consideration the past motion of a reference picture, a prediction value representing the motion of a current picture by comparing the current picture itself with the reference picture. In either case, it is desirable that an algorithm for encoding a moving picture is simple and can highly express the shape, the texture and the quality of the picture and that the amount of information required thereby be reduced. Transcutaneous electrical brain stimulation (TEC) using the "A" or "F" electrode configuration has been developed to provide a controlled electrical field in the spinal cord by means of surface electrodes. A few case reports have demonstrated effectiveness from TEC in the treatment of essential tremor (ET). Previous clinical studies have used TEC/FEP to increase the amplitude of the resting tremor as well as to alleviate pre-existing tremor. In addition, TEC/FEP has been shown to be an effective adjunct to other therapies. However, the role of TEC/FEP as an early treatment of ET has not been systematically investigated. Sixteen ET patients were treated using TEC/FEP for a 3-5 day duration. The effectiveness and tolerability of TEC/FEP was evaluated in all subjects prior to treatment and at 4, 15, 30 and 45 minutes post-treatment. <|endoftext|>Aeration systems generally fall into two categories, gas or liquid. Aerating systems employed in the medical field today have gas- or liquid-based systems. Gas-based systems often use compressed air or inert gas as the medium, although other gases such as air, neon, or the like have also been used.
More complex liquid-based systems are often known as continuous flow systems. In a continuous flow system, the liquid is typically drawn or sprayed onto a liquid-bearing surface such as a floor, table, bed, or other surface. The liquid is then removed by vacuum or the like. In an industrial vacuum system, a vacuum creates a vacuum in a tube that draws the liquid by suction therefrom. A liquid-based vacuum system may also use a surface coating, film, or other type of carrier to form a wick on the surface and hold the liquid.
In a medical treatment application, continuous flow aeration systems are used in conjunction with a vacuum system to generate a hypobaric chamber. The hypobaric chamber is, by definition, more stable than ordinary atmospheric pressure, thus permitting a patient to breath more freely. CREATE TABLE `new_table` (
  `id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `name` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`),
  UNIQUE KEY `name_unique` (`name`)
) ENGINE=MyISAM  DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8

2. Description of the Related Art
In general, a class-D amplifier circuit is a power amplifier with a gain of several tens dB higher than a conventional class-AB amplifier circuit. Specifically, this amplifying circuit has a large non-linear characteristic with a comparatively small variation, where a power gain Gm of the amplifier is expressed in the following formula,
Gm=[Gm(1/R)−1]1/2  (equation 1)
In the equation 1, R represents a power supply voltage, and Gm represents gain of the amplifier for each power supply voltage. Thus, by controlling a value of R, the gain of the amplifier can be continuously changed along an ideal output signal frequency characteristic as shown in the figure.

<|endoftext|>A review of the literature of the role of platelet derived growth factors and vascular cells in atherosclerosis.
Platelets play a pivotal role in the pathogenesis of atheroma. Recent biochemical and morphologic studies have demonstrated that atherothrombosis occurs as a consequence of a complex cascade involving the platelets, vascular smooth muscle cells and macrophages which leads to the formation of a lesion within the arterial tree. This article reviews the relevant literature of platelet derived growth factors and vascular cells in the formation and progression of atherosclerosis. Experimental evidence suggests that a possible mechanistic connection exists between platelet derived growth factors and atherosclerosis.<|endoftext|>

<|endoftext|>The invention relates to a method for transmitting signals with a digital-link carrier, in which method information data to be transmitted are converted into digital signals and the digital signals are modulated onto a digital-link carrier.
In the prior art such signals are generally transmitted over copper cables or over optical fiber cables. The digital signals take the form of information data to be transmitted on the digital-link carrier, these digital signals being modulated onto the digital-link carrier by a modulator. Thus, the modulator generates the corresponding digital signals and transmits them in a manner in which these digital signals can be received. The modulator is preferably constructed as an integrated circuit, since this makes it possible to provide the modulator in a correspondingly smaller space and at an even shorter assembly and maintenance cost.
The information data are, for example, audio signals, which are transmitted over a telephone line and are converted into digital signals and transmitted over an optical fiber. However the information data are also transmitted using two carriers. In this case, a pilot signal is transmitted at a very low frequency from a central station and a data signal is transmitted at a higher frequency. Therefore, the information data being transmitted are transmitted over two different carriers, for both information signals and pilot signals.

<|endoftext|>The present invention relates generally to an electrical connector assembly, and more particularly to an electrical connector assembly that facilitates the alignment of individual signal contacts within an electrical connector.
Electrical connector assemblies are common in the electronics industry and typically include an electrical connector and a plurality of electrical contact elements for receiving and/or transmitting electrical signals. The electrical connector is used to connect an integrated circuit to a corresponding printed circuit board or to a complementary connector on another circuit board. Generally, the electrical signal lines are connected from the integrated circuit to the electrical contact elements at different electrical connector terminals that are mounted on the printed circuit board(s). It, however, is also known to provide integrated circuits that have electrically isolated contact areas to transmit discrete signals.
The electrical connectors typically are of the types known as quad flat or ball grid array (QFH) connectors that comprise four rows of generally parallel signal contact elements. The individual signal contact elements are in turn coupled in some manner to a common electrical backplane that typically incorporates a plurality of circuit boards and electrical connectors.
Some integrated circuits have a plurality of signal contact elements that are placed in different layers or planes. Such integrated circuits are known in the art as multiple-layer or 3D integrated circuits and in some respects are similar to quad

A total of 857 specimens belonging to three major Chinese species of sea cucumbers, including two species of Echinulinatus, Echinulinatichthys meinerti and Echinulinatus kumandayensis (both belonging to the Echinulinatidae), and Echinulinatus griffini was collected on the beaches of Hong Kong and Fujian, respectively. Each species contained three morphotypes which were collected mainly from different zones of both regions. Comparative morphometric studies using conventional indices of shape, form and relative dimensions revealed that the species with the largest body size was E. kumandayensis, while E. meinerti was the smallest. The species with the smallest body size was E. griffini. Statistical analyses of the relative dimensions and three types of shape indices revealed the species with the highest levels of variation to be E. kumandayensis and E. meinerti, while the E. griffini had the lowest variation in body size and relative dimensions. <|endoftext|>Q:

How to avoid error "Invalid or no permission to view data on database" in Visual Studio?

When I'm trying to publish application in Visual Studio 2010 I get exception in table designer: ERROR 0X6000043: Invalid or no permission to view data on database.
My problem is that I don't have permissions, I forgot these permissions since few months and now I don't want to renew them. How can I solve such errors?
Thanks.

A:

When you don't have permissions, then the database is not actually the problem, but a database driver or application user could be the problem.
You need to be certain that you have permissions on the database and that a user doesn't have permissions.
As @NabeelBassil says, it is important that the database in question has a login associated with it.

Spirae () is a 2016 French-Maltese drama film directed by Jean-Marc Berny. The film premiered in Cannes' Directors' Fortnight section, competing with other projects like Amélie by Emmanuel Moll, The Kid Who Is Not There by John Michael McDonagh, and I'm Your Man by Michael Winterbottom. Its world premiere was at the Pordenone International Film Festival in June 2016 and screened at other festivals in July and August 2016. Spirae won the David di Donatello Award in October 2016. <|endoftext|>Tropical Medicine in the United States

Tropical medicine is increasingly used in the United States. Patients are increasingly choosing tropical medicine clinics to help manage and treat their disease. Here are five reasons why this is important:

1) Tropical health practices are inexpensive and very accessible. Most costs are far lower than traditional health practices, making it easy to afford them.

2) Because the focus is on the specific disease being treated, a patient can take more control of their condition.

3) A tropical medicine clinic can help the patient’s whole family to better manage, or even cure, their disease.

4) A tropical medicine clinic can be a long-term investment, helping to save the lives of patients as the disease progresses and becomes severe.

5) Tropical medicine practices often incorporate the use of herbal medicines and alternative medicine.

If you are a US resident looking for a treatment option in tropical medicine, it is useful to know how some are regulated in the United States.

<|endoftext|>[Evaluation of health and quality of life in patients undergoing coronary bypass surgery according to the SF-36].

How to find and replace a double quote inside of a string in Vim?

I have the below string:
str = 'This is a String with double quotes'

It was easy as doing the following:
:%s/\'/\\'/g

But when I added a double quote then it would complain that string contains some control quote:
:%s/\"\"/\\\\\"/g

So now how could I find and replace a double quote inside of a string with Vim?

A:

In gVim 2.0 and later the double-quote in the middle of you string does not need to be escaped. You can either add the double-quote to your pattern (e.g. :%s/\'/\'/g or :%s/\"\'/\'/g) or use a more general pattern (e.g. the regex '\(.*\)/\1/g).
For gVim 1.0 and earlier, the double-quote on the character sequence \ and inside of the \'s has to be escaped as \'. Also, you have to escape the closing quote, so e.g. :%

<|endoftext|>The invention relates to a method for driving a plasma display panel (hereinafter abbreviated to PDP) using an integrated circuit for driving.
The use of a liquid crystal display, a plasma display panel and so on have gained rapidly popularity in recent years because they have the characteristic that they do not need to be observed directly from the outside. Generally speaking, PDPs which have low power dissipation are driven by a small power source, such as a dry cell or a small battery used as an integrated circuit for driving the PDP. In the prior art, the power consumption within PDP drivers is reduced by employing the method for reducing the supply clock frequency of an integrate circuit for driving a PDP while increasing the number of scanning electrodes in the same line. In order to implement this function, PDP drive integrated circuits employ PDP analog to digital converters having large size.

The study design consisted of cross-sectional analysis of each 2 groups and of 3 randomized crossover studies of GH replacement (hGH 1.0 units/day) for 10 months and for 2 months and hGH 0.33 units/day with or without an optional crossover treatment period of 2 months each. <|endoftext|>Q:

Why isn't the last letter of a word the same as it is written

Why, when we spell out a name, are we so careful to make sure that the last name (or surname) spellings match the last letter of the spelling, as if to make sure that the person is still named in the same way? 
This was an extremely annoying problem to me as a child. As a result, I learned that I was supposed to match the spelling by making sure that it was at the very end of the word (the letter after the middle letter of the word). So in other words, if the word was "John Smith" it should be spelled out "John smith", not "John SMITH". 
And of course, there is a reason why it is this way and it's not to bother anybody. It's for privacy, it's a way for people to check that that name is really really really really really quite so, not some other name. 
What I do think is, though, is that it's a bit daft that in this digital age of electronic information, people who had this name before the internet were quite likely to look up their name and make sure that it was correct

<|endoftext|>[A clinical and experimental study of the effects of nisoldipine on vasospastic cerebral circulation in migraine with aura].
Nisoldipine, a calcium entry blocker, was given to 30 migraine without aura, 6 patients with migraine with aura and 18 healthy, neurologically normal subjects for 10 days, during the attack phase. The following results were obtained 1) Headache scores were significantly reduced (p less than 0.01) following treatment with nisoldipine, although they remained higher than those found during the headache phase. 2) At the end of the first course, migraine with aura patients had significantly improved scores throughout the two phases of the migraine with aura compared with those of both migraine without aura and migraine patients whose head pains were not treated with nisoldipine. 3) Nisoldipine did not suppress mean arterial pressure (MAP) and did not affect heart rate compared with those which were not treated with nisoldipine in the migraine with aura patients or in those whose head pains were treated with nisoldipine. These results suggest that nisoldipine produced beneficial effects on migraine with aura by its antiarrhythmic and vasodilator activities.<|endoftext|>

<|endoftext|>Q:

Get the class or package name inside a web project

Im trying to get the class or package name from inside a php project using CodeAnalysis,
but it is returning something like this:

PHP Code Analysis warning: class Webpage\Test\Controller\DashboardController is not autoloaded
  class Webpage\Test\Controller\DashboardController
  in vendor/webpage/test/Controller.php

So how can i get the name of Webpage\Test\ because i would like to get 
the class or package name with out the prefix

A:

Use Fileinfo or Composer's composer.php to get the actual name of the file. The only reason you don't find the 'Package' (or even class_name) is because you use the webbrowser() function and so you don't care about the names of the files and files included in the project. In most cases web browser() uses some namespaces, but in very rare cases that is not the case and then "Package" or "Class" is in fact not a well-defined word.
Edit: if you don't use webbrowser(); then no need to use Composer.

<|endoftext|>Q:

What's the use on an object in an ArrayList from a Java method?

I create the ArrayList of object A, then pass the ArrayList object to a method, then use the method to assign a object of type B to the ArrayList object. What's the use of using ArrayList from the A object in the A method? Why can't I use one ArrayList from an object directly? For example:
public class A
{
   public void printArray(ArrayList<B> list){...}

Here it means list.add(new B()); what's the good use of the ArrayList<B>?

A:

You've got multiple objects of the same type in the ArrayList. Therefore, Java tries to avoid copying data between them unless all the data elements of different types are treated as one object (a generic reference to an external data structure). The ArrayList is a data structure which makes it easy to keep the right amount of data.
You can either use the ListIterator which takes a Collection and returns one object of the same type at a time (allowing you to use ArrayList), or you can make a generic collection. There are several libraries

<|endoftext|>The effect of recombinant human growth hormone on bone mass of children born small for gestational age with and without insulin-like growth factor-I deficiency.
To investigate the relationship between small for gestational age (SGA) and insulin-like growth factor-I (IGF-I), 20 cases with SGA with IGF-I deficiency and 14 cases with SGA with normal IGF-I (control) were studied. Three groups were compared in terms of bone mineral content (BMC) before and after (from one to four years of age) the administration of human recombinant (rh) GH (0.2 U at each dose) by a growth hormone receptor-antibody (GHRA) method. In the cases who received rhGH, the percentage of BMC decrease and the percentage of BMC increase were significantly smaller than in cases who did not receive rhGH, and BMC increased in the former group after the administration of rhGH was significantly higher than in the latter group after the rhGH was stopped, and the percentage of BMC increase thereafter was significantly higher in the former group than in the latter group. <|endoftext|>Q:

How do I get all elements under the current viewport in WebKit?

If there are elements in the current viewport, how can one retrieve all of them?

A:

If there are elements in the current viewport, how can one retrieve all of them?
This is not possible in WebKit/Apple because there is no real viewport. It's only a thing you build your frames around. But it's possible in Safari.

For reference: You build your frames around the viewport you'd otherwise have to put the page in front of. E.g.: You use the full-screen API to make the viewport larger to cover whatever is behind it and then use the scrollbars to scroll from left to right so you can see through it.
Also note that the viewport is often (but not always) visible while drawing the background to the screen.
So, a solution to the question you posed is: Build your frame around the screen instead of within it, like this:
http://jsfiddle.net/jfriend00/7k6xkpjd/10/
html, body {
    margin: 0;
}

Bundle bundle = new Bundle();
bundle.putString(FacebookConstants.API_VERSION_KEY, (int) version);

then to make an app for any version of API:
final Intent intent=new Intent (this, MainActivity.class);
intent.putExtras(bundle); //put the bundle on the intent.

IEnumerable Enqueue() overloads

If I have the following interface...
IEnumerable<T>

...which is backed by an IEnumerable<T> collection, would I still need to provide both the foreach() and the sort() overloads?  Or can I get away with hardwiring the two for now and get away with just providing one...
foreach(List<T> list in Enqueue<IEnumerable<T>>())

Thanks!

A:

Enqueue and Dequeue have overloads to Enqueue() and Dequeue()
You need to also pass in a delegate like this to get the sort
Enqueue(IEnumerable<T> list, Func<IEnumerable<T>,IEnumerable<T>,bool> sortMethod)

A:

It is fine to leave the foreach(List<T> list in Enqueue<IEnumerable<T>>()) in place.  It just calls the sort method of the provided delegate (which you still want) but it also calls Enqueue(Enumerable, sortMethod) which is just fine too.

Get an array out of stdin

I'm using
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>

and I've run into this problem:
I get from stdin an array of strings. I don't want it to be in an array. How can I get it to an array when using the stdin function?

A:

What stdin function you use (with in as its standard input stream) will create a null-terminated array. You could use fgets instead or, when you just want to read from stdin:
    size_t n = 1;
    char data[n] = {0};
    fgets(data, sizeof data, stdin);

A:

You can't. stdin is just a port of the C library functions and there's no such thing as the array type 'a[b]'. When you use fgets() on stdin what you get is a null-terminated string with the size of the buffer you read, so you'd get a string of the following type:
char string[3] = "Hello";

filetype.vim

<|endoftext|>Q:

Prove inequality using definition of sum and mean

Let $a_1,a_2,\dots a_n$ be a sequence of nonnegative real numbers. Prove that
$$
\sum_{i=1}^k a_i\geq \frac{k^3}{3}a_{k+1}.
$$

A:

Suppose $k\geqslant a_{k+1}-1$. Then
$$
\sum_{i=1}^k (a_i+1)=\sum_{i=1}^{k+1} (a_i+1)\geqslant \left(\frac{k+1}{2+(k-1)}a_{k+1}+1\right)(k+1)\geqslant \frac{k^3}{3}a_{k+1},
$$ since
$$
a_{k+1}=\big(1+\frac{k^2}{6}\big)\frac{k^3}{3}.
$$

<|endoftext|>Q:

What's the difference between a regular expression and a Regex in R?

I have come across two versions of the syntax:

[ ]   - 'any single character'
\[   - 'any single character' 
[^ ]  - 'a single character that is NOT a '.'

I understand the first one but the second one i don't understand. Is it the same as a negated character class ?

A:

Both of your character classes have a meaning. ^ is a negated character class, [^ ] is a negated character class, and [], {} are not characters, and the latter, {} are a set of characters. Using character classes is, in fact, equivalent to using the negated character classes, [a-z], {^}, [a-z].
So [^ ] and [{^}, {^}] really do mean the same thing, and these are commonly used to create matchers that will match a sub-sequence of characters in the string being checked to see if it matches the specified pattern (regex).
By using the character class approach (using [^ ]) the pattern will not match whitespace

<|endoftext|>Q:

What are the different ways to use GIT as a version control system for python programming?

What are the different ways to use GIT as a version control system for python programming?

A:

I just started to learn git and I'm going to start off by telling you why I like it more than SVN, but it's also a very good option:

git is very fast:

In a very typical web project, you spend 90% of your time doing:

git update and pull
git add .
git commit -m '...'

It takes 30 seconds to get a pull, while SVN would take 45 minutes.
You also get an actual commit message of your choice as a commit ID.

There are other advantages, but the two are obvious.
But for all these reasons, I think git is a superior option.

<|endoftext|>Numerous techniques and/or devices are commercially available for the in situ generation of electricity including wind turbines for the conversion of wind energy into usable electrical power. A typical wind turbine includes a tower having a generator and a rotor positioned thereon. The rotor may be driven by a rotor shaft that, in turn, is mounted to the hub of a drive shaft that is connected to electric generators that in turn provide electrical power to a utility grid. The wind that blows through the blades of the turbine is transformed to a rotational torque (e. a. torque) that drives the rotor. The rotor of conventional wind turbines in the horizontal position is horizontal and therefore rotates with little momentum. In the vertical position, the rotor operates at a substantial fraction of the speed of the rotational wind, thereby providing for increased efficiency. The tower of a wind turbine also facilitates the generation of electrical power, and the generation of electrical power is achieved by accelerating the wind in the tower and accelerating the rotor of the wind turbine in a counteracting direction.
It is desirable that such a wind turbine be self-sufficient and be capable of operation in multiple rotational positions without requiring significant maintenance or servicing of the drive shaft or other mechanical components thereof. It is also desirable to be able to easily

<|endoftext|>Nuclear receptor superfamily consists of proteins which regulate transcription using both binding interactions outside and within their own DNA. NURF members function in the nucleus with many other nuclear proteins to control and coordinate these steps of transcription, as well as a specific transcriptional response. The NURF family may be related to the basic helix-loop-helix (bHLH) proteins (B-bHLH) or to the helix-loop-helix proteins (L-HLH) from different organisms and to the homeodomain proteins (HOM) from animals. The gene families are characterized by different number of members and by subfamily names and a general division of family members into two groups was postulated. The present investigation of NURF genes includes the analysis of the gene structure and expression of NURF family members in several different tissues and during ontogeny. Differential expression of NURF proteins was found by Northern blot, by PCR and by immunocytochemistry. Northern blot hybridization with genes encoding the different NURF family members revealed that the 5 genes identified from our library were transcribed at different rates in different tissues, in particular in the liver and in the brain. <|endoftext|>Aminoacyl-tRNA synthetases (aaRS) are the key enzymes responsible for attaching amino acids to their cognate tRNAs in living cells. These two-enzyme systems are vital for the translation of DNA into proteins, where they are responsible for bringing the information encoded in the genetic code to three levels: (1) synthesis of the cognate amino acid based on the Watson-Crick base pairing of the anticodon with mRNA codon; (2) transfer from the aminoacyl-transfer ribonucleoprotein (ATP-ribosyltransferase) into the cognate tRNA followed by aminoacylation by the aminoacyl-tRNA synthetase; and (3) joining of two tRNAs together. To improve amino acid and tRNA synthetase function as well as their application as molecular tools, it is essential to understand how the functions of the two enzymes are related. Our long-term goal is to understand the relationships between amino acid and tRNA synthetase, as this leads to the generation of new protein function mutants with improved functions. <|endoftext|>[Clinical analysis based on clinical data from 6 cases of bilateral adrenocortical phaeochromocytomas].
Objective: To analyze the clinical characteristics of patients with bilateral adrenocortical phaeochromocytomas. Methods: Six patients were treated at the Department of Endocrinology and Metabolism of Xijing Hospital from 2001 to 2017 were retrospectively reviewed for inclusion in the current research. The clinical characteristics were summarized. Results: The median ages (months) of these patients at the time of presentation was 11.0 (1-64) months. Five of the patients had no underlying diseases. Four of the patients were male. One presented with increased blood pressure and one with hypertension without other presenting symptoms. The tumor sizes were 0.6-2.9 cm at diagnosis and 12-25.5 cm 3 years later. Two presented with hypertension and one with weight loss. All had hypertension and symptoms 3-6 months later when the tumor size was larger than 25.5 cm. Conclusions: The clinical presentation is highly variable and therefore needs close follow up. <|endoftext|>Molecular mechanisms of neuronal plasticity induced by brain-derived neurotrophic factor and serotonin.
Degenerative and neuritic alterations in the mammalian central nervous system are characterized by both hyperactive and atrophic processes. Neurotrophic factors play a role in synaptic organization, by activating different neuroprotective and regeneration systems, as well as in the control of cell death in neurons and glial cells. These factors can induce neuronal differentiation at least in part by regulating the expression of several neurosteroidogenic genes. In addition, neuronal factors may modify neurotransmitter expression, ionic and metabolic transport, signal transduction cascades, the production and turnover of certain neurotrophic molecules and proteins, synaptic architecture and, also, the expression of neurodevelopmentally relevant genes. We have focused on brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) which is a member of the neurotrophin family and the most potent growth factor described for the central nervous system. BDNF is a protein with widespread actions during postnatal and adult life and acts on at least 2 types of effectors: the receptor tyrosine kinases TrkB and p75NTR, as well as on transcription factors belonging to the serum response factor (SRF) and serum and glucocorticoid

Is there a reason why, if some people die and others just seem 'to live'?

A:

You should first ask yourself, if I had an intelligent conversation with you, would I understand your situation?

But to answer your question, consider that we exist in the same way that other creatures exist. We are not a different species, we are of the same species as that other species. We have a body, it consumes the food and oxygen that humans consume in order to live, we breathe, we excrete, we digest, we make babies, etc and so on.
Therefore we exist because humans make the same life decisions as other humans. For example, we choose whether or not to eat. They choose if they will eat, and what they eat. In both cases they do so because they have a life-preserving drive to do so.
So we are not alive because we decide to eat or breathe or excrete

<|endoftext|>The objective of the research proposed here is to characterize the role of the cytoplasmic domain (CD) of the mouse mammary tumor virus (MMTV) oncoprotein in the inhibition of mammary tumor formation in MMTV-induced mouse mammary tumor virus (MMTV-MuMTV) transgenic mice. The CD will be used to create fusion proteins containing the CD from the MMTV-MuMTV gene and genes which contain mutations known to disrupt protein function and/or to affect growth mechanisms. The mutant proteins will be expressed on the MMTV CD and the resulting proteins will be immunologically shown to retain the CD function. The fusion proteins will also be tested for growth inhibitory activity in vivo and for their capacity to form complexes in vitro. <|endoftext|>Molecular detection of BRCA germline mutations in breast and ovarian-type cancers using PCR-based technologies.
The ability to sequence a human coding region for somatic BRCA mutations can provide critical information about potential cancer susceptibility for many BRCA-associated breast and ovarian-type cancers as well as hereditary breast and ovarian cancer. While the molecular technologies used for both research and clinical sequencing have improved, the ability to provide complete coding sequence for breast and ovarian cancers with known mutations and their potential carriers is still not routine practice. A range of approaches is currently employed in most laboratories working in this area to provide the molecular diagnostic information required for these cases within a time frame that may exceed the clinical outcome of the subject. One of the more innovative approaches employed is an assay for detection of somatic mutations in these cancers that have previously been shown to be associated with the specific phenotype. The current molecular diagnostic methods provide a range of mutation detection techniques for BRCA-associated breast and ovarian cancer, but these assays do not provide the sequence information necessary to make inferences about patient risk or carrier status. MySql: How do I query for more than two values in one column between two dates?

I have a table with data in it. It has the following info.

The column called "OrderDate" has either 2012-12-13 or 2012-12-12 as its value. That column and the others are unique for that order.
So for example, if the user has ordered 2012-12-13, I want to know which other orders happened after that one that is also possible.
In this situation I want either 2012-12-13 or 2012-12-14.

A:

This can be achieved with a subquery:
select *
from (
    select x.*
    from x order by x.order_id
    limit 2
) t
where date(t.order_date) <= str_to_date(date(x.order_date) + interval 1 day, '%Y-%m-%d');

Note that str_to_date is an extension function that replaces %Y with the day of month and %m with the month name, so the query above does not

<|endoftext|>[Prognostic value of serum soluble interleukin 2 receptor assay in metastatic melanoma patients].
Soluble interleukin 2 receptor (SII) assay has recently been introduced as a useful tool in the diagnosis and assessment of biological markers involved in the course of malignant diseases. This study aimed to assess the value of measuring SII in patients with metastatic melanoma by using a standardized protocol in order to determine its prognostic reliability. The patients included in the study were treated on the basis of a prospective protocol in which the serum of the patients was collected at the moment of progression being at a stage of measurable metastases. Patients who survived 2 months or longer since first onset of metastases were qualified for the investigation. Soluble interleukin 2 receptor concentration before treatment was found to be significantly higher in the patients with a poor survival. This fact indicates that measurement of SII in the serum may provide an index of a poor prognosis. The study also demonstrated that the serum SII level before treatment is a strong prognostic indicator, in this case reflecting the early prognosis (survival by 2 years in 94% of the patients with a prognostic score above 2.9 SD) of the metastatic melanoma patients.<|endoftext|>

He was drafted by the Philadelphia Eagles in the 8th round (186th overall) of the 1975 NFL Draft.  In 2000, he became the first player in NFL history to have his name appear as a full member of the NFL Hall of Fame.

<|endoftext|>The development of resistance to HIV-1 is a growing concern. Many clinical studies demonstrated that it is possible to develop resistance to HIV-1, which usually results in disease progression. The most prevalent mechanism in HIV-1 resistance is to target viral proteins that are essential for HIV-1 replication, which leads to a reduction in viral production, replication of the provirus, and, eventually, the inability of the virus to make its genetic mark on host cells.[@R1]

Resistance may emerge soon after viral infection or may emerge later, as a result of drug use or HIV-1 treatment. Resistance to protease (PR) inhibitors was the first resistance observed in patients who started taking a protease inhibitor (PIs) within three months of their HIV-1 infection. PR inhibitor resistance mutations had been observed to emerge early in ART.[@R2]^-^[@R4] The appearance of PR inhibitor resistance mutations, which is commonly associated with reduced viral inhibition by PR inhibitors, could therefore allow replication of the provirus to continue. <|endoftext|>The long term objective of this proposal is to determine the role of the cytoskeleton in regulating normal and altered neuronal cell function and to investigate the potential utility of cytoskeletal targeting agents to alter the biological function of neurocytes. The neurocyte is a specialized cell type in the mammalian olfactory system which, like most central nervous system (CNS) neurons, is capable of multiple functional changes. Since neurocytes are the sole source of olfactory information processing in the mammalian CNS, alterations to the neurocyte function could have a pervasive affect on the entire sensory neural system. For example, a deficit in sensory processing could lead to decreased olfactory detection and olfactory memory formation. Alternatively, a dysfunction could result in an inability to respond appropriately to olfactory stimuli if a normally functioning neurocyte fails to exhibit a characteristic behavior. The proposed research will investigate three fundamental questions concerning the normal function of the neurocyte: a) What is the normal function of the neurocyte? b) What are the functional changes that result from manipulations of the neurocyte? c) What are the functional characteristics of the neurocyte that are necessary for its normal function and those that are not? <|endoftext|>Q:

What does "npm install" actually do?

When you execute node node-v11.4.0, it install many modules from that version, and when you execute npm install, it's actually installing packages, it's just like installing node packages?

A:

This is what npm install and yarn install do.
npm install will install the packages you've specified in your package.json file.
yarn will download all the dependencies for you. If you leave off the --ignore-scripts flag, it'll run scripts with your current working directory set as its current working directory.
This basically just sets the package.json package to node_modules/ and then installs all modules from those modules.
The --ignore-scripts flag will force npm to run the scripts specified in package.json in your current working directory. 
If you use this flag, you can also remove the package.json file. But that's not recommended because you don't really know what's being installed from package.js if you haven't explicitly installed it.
This means that, if you do: npm install --ignore-scripts Ionic@3.1.0 --save, you could have multiple I

<|endoftext|>Q:

How to run a python script in background so it doesn't freeze?

At the moment I have something like this so it doesn't freeze the whole program, unless I leave it.
import os
import subprocess
import time

path='/home/user/Desktop/Desktop'
subprocess.Popen(['python', 'test.py'])
time.sleep(60)
os.chdir(path)

Is there any other way I can run a script in background without freezing the whole program?

A:

Just schedule the job:
subprocess.Popen(['python', 'test.py'], shell=True, create_sig=False)

For full documentation, see Subprocess.Popen's description.
Edit If you plan to use the same command throughout the program, you can put the job right before the loop:
subprocess.Popen(['python', 'test.py'], shell=True, create_sig=False)
time.sleep(60)
os.chdir(path)

The job will run in the background. If you want an equivalent script with a fixed directory

<|endoftext|>Ceiling fans exist for many different reasons, one of which is to enhance the airflow and cooling of the room in which they are installed. Although this function is desirable, ceiling fans are usually restricted in their ability to circulate air in the ceiling region due to the ceiling's orientation and the shape of the ceiling fan blades.
Ceiling fans have been designed utilizing curved blades to direct airflow in a more efficient manner. As shown in U.S. Pat. No. 5,724,731, a fan blade assembly is fixed to a rigid arm. In order to support the blade in a horizontal orientation in the wall, the rigid arm must have sufficient dimensions. Unfortunately, the additional wall space and the additional support structure needed to maintain the horizontal orientation of the blade within the wall increases the overall height of the fan assembly and creates obstructions to airflow from the room to which the fan is positioned. In addition, any changes in the room's orientation of the ceiling fan require the use of additional hardware to ensure that the blade fits within the desired orientation. In those situations where the room to which the assembly is mounted is smaller than the size of the fan blade assembly, the structure of the assembly must be modified to accommodate the reduced sized room space.

Breast cancer is a disease of the elderly. In the sentence, "I'm sure it's not my fault", it's clear that "I'm 100% certain" is what they're referring to.
"I am 100% certain that it's not my fault" or something similar, would have been a better way to express the same point, because it implies a certain level of certainty that they're expressing.
In general though, "not sure" doesn't mean "I am not 100% certain". "not sure about it" means that you are uncertain about whether something happened or not, but you are certain enough to make it clear that you don't think the event was due to anything in your power (or anyone's, for that matter).

Why does the `if` statement work in my program?

I have this code that works perfectly:
if ((i % 2)==0){
printf("%u is even, and is a palindrome.",i); } else {
printf(i); }

However, when I add the else clause, it no longer works. Why is that?

A:

You're comparing i with the number 2 in the wrong way.
The correct way would be to do i = i % 2;, because the modulus operator only works with integers and not floating point values.
The reason you get unexpected results is because you're printing out the value of i before you're comparing i.
The correct code is:
if ((i == 2) || (i%2==0 )){
printf("%lu is even,  and is a palindrome.",i); }
else {
                   printf(i); }

<|endoftext|>A comparison of two methods for the measurement of the left-right difference in cardiac output.
A new technique has been devised for the measurement of the cardiac left-right difference in patients with normal cardiac function. The method is based on simultaneous measurement by the thermodiluter and a pulmonary artery flow meter, of the left-right differences in cardiac output (Q). In the thermodiluter, the pulmonary artery and the left ventricular outfist are occluded. In the pulmonary artery flowmeter, the left and right ventricular outfist are occluded. The left ventricular pressure and cardiac output are measured simultaneously in each occluded ventricle. This method has been compared with the thermodilutertest and the thermodilutertest with a modified pulmonary artery flowmeter in 10 normal subjects (age 24-40) and 8 cardiac transplant recipients (age 23-39). In the normal subjects and transplant subjects studied, the thermodilutertest was more accurate than the thermodilutertest with a modified pulmonary artery flowmeter in measuring Q and in determining the left-right difference in cardiac output. In both groups, the mean differences were less than 2%, and the limits of agreement were +/-

<|endoftext|>The effect of age on the development and outcome of acute myocardial infarction.
In order to evaluate the role of age in the development and prognosis of acute myocardial infarction (MI) we compared patients with MI aged 30 or less, aged 41 to 59 or aged 60 or more to patients without MI, in a retrospective analysis from a large coronary care unit. There were 6,890 admissions during the 6 year period of the study (1980 to 1982). The age of the patients with MI (n=2,621) was significantly greater in comparison to the patients without acute MI (n. 2,890). The age-group with MI was more often male, was more likely to have an acute myocardial infarction of anterior location and less likely to have angina pectoris. Patients who survived MI (n = 2,721) were more likely to have been admitted in an emergency ward and were more likely to have had a prior infarct. There were more deaths in patients with MI compared with patients without MI and patients with MI were more likely to have a prior myocardial infarction. The age-adjusted relative risk of death was greater in patients aged less than 40 years

Alfred John Foyt (July 1, 1888, New York City – June 11, 1964, Los Angeles) was an American football coach, and college athletics administrator. Foyt served as the head baseball, football and baseball coach at Occidental College (1919–1923), the University of Oregon (1923–1927), and the University of Southern California (1927–1935). He also served as the head football coach at the College of the Pacific (1927–1929), and at the California Institute of Technology (1929–1933) and at the University of Southern California (1933), compiling a career college baseball record of 118 games, and a total of 472 wins. He served as the head football coach at Occidental from 1919 to 1923 and at USC from 1923 to 1935, compiling an 18–21–2 overall coaching record. He was the head baseball coach and athletic director at the University of California from 1939 to 1943. 

After leaving USC in 1935, he served as the athletic director at Occidental College until 1946 and was the head football coach there from 1946 to 1951.  Foyt died on June 11, 1964 at his home

<|endoftext|>The effect of the number and type of exercise training sessions on the development of the aerobic fitness test in young men.
The aim of this research study was to examine the effect of the frequency of training sessions, the duration and intensity of training and the type of training (aerobic vs. anaerobic) on the development of aerobic fitness. Thirty-four young male subjects performed the following four training programmes: a) three times a wk, b) four times a wk, c) five times a wk, d) six times a wk. The results of the present investigation indicated that the aerobic fitness of these young men improved in a similar degree after all the four training programmes. The aerobic fitness was found to be positively correlated to both the frequency of exercise and the duration of exercise. However, the intensity of the training programme was not found to be related to the improvement of aerobic fitness. How to get a list of files with their last modification date in bash?
(I know there must exist something that is more easy to implement but I am a newbie in Linux and Bash.)

A:  You can use find to do this, but you can do it much better with GNU find and the -printf '%F\n' or -printf '%p: \t%t\n' switches:
$ find . -type f | grep -v "^$" | xargs -L 1 -0 ls -lh -1m -t | tail -n1

-L 1 is the length of the first argument. -1m means "show me the modification date of all files, even those with the same name as the current directory" and -h is for the "human readable" format, so that you don't have to read through all of the output and then parse the dates with awk.
The -0 option means "skip files whose name doesn't begin with a dot (.)". -0 means to "skip files whose name is the name of another directory or file".

<|endoftext|>A new study from the National Institutes of Health (NIH) and the University of California San Francisco shows that when a mother’s diet changes, her children’s weight can change in the opposite direction.

The researchers studied the relationship between diet and children in the Nurses’ Health Study II, a large study of nurses.

Researchers followed the children’s weight from age 3 and tracked it from birth to age 10, measuring weight every two to three years. The researchers analyzed the diet of mother and children.

Mothers in the highest category of adherence had children who were 0.4-0.7 kilograms heavier on average than children with mothers in the lowest category, the researchers found.

“It’s not that the mother’s diet was responsible,” Dr. Jennifer Doud, lead author on the study, told NBC News MACH in a phone interview. “It was how that diet impacted her child’s weight.”

The children’s diet did not influence how much the parents ate, according to a statement released by the researchers.

The findings, however, may have broader implications than simply the impact on children.

<|endoftext|>Q: How to use the `--` argument in the `find` command?

How can I use the -- argument for find?

A:

You can use the -- in the find command as follows:
find . -regextype posix-egrep -regex ".*\[\s*(?:[^\[]*[^\[]*\[\s]*)\]*.*"

This is a simple example.  You can also use this in a script:
for f in *.txt; do
  find . -regextype posix-egrep -regex ".*\[\s*(?:[^\]]*[^\]]*\[\s]*)\]*" $f
  # do something with file $f

  done

The -regextype flag is a GNU extension.  It specifies that the find command should return files whose names are not just a list of words separated by spaces but instead have a regular-expression type (egrep) or a list of glob-like expressions (egrep -l).
The $f variable is a variable name for the file being examined

<|endoftext|>Q:

What happens when I call the method of the same object from a different class?

I am trying to learn about object-oriented programming in Java and was reading about inheritance.
In the book, it is mentioned that when a method of a child class is called from a method of the parent class, the method of the child will be called first and then the method of the same object from the parent. 
My questions are: Is it true?   If it is true, how is this different from what the book is describing?  
Also, I know it will be very difficult to understand, but I would like to know if there is a simple example of the same thing.

A:

The parent method is always the first in order of invocation, so the first child method will be called when the parent's method call. 
This is different from what the book is describing, because the book doesn't say the parent's method call is always the first in order of execution.  
For example, if I call a child method from a parent, then I can also do the reverse - call a parent method from a child method, but the order is not guaranteed.

Carboxylesterase (EC 3.1.1, CE) is involved with the metabolism of various drugs in the liver. The effect of an oral contraceptive on the activity of this enzyme in the liver was studied in ovariosed female Wistar rats. The enzyme activity in the ovariosed rats was significantly higher as compared to the normal female rats. In ovariosectomized rats, the enzyme activity was not significantly different between control and ovariosed rats. The effect of oral contraceptives on serum levels of the enzyme were investigated. The results showed that the oral contraceptives significantly lowered the serum levels of carboxylesterase, as compared with the control group. <|endoftext|>Q:

Can I get the name of the current file in the shell?

I am using the GNU screen utility in the shell, but I would like to be able to get the name of the currently open file.
For example:
screen
screen -r

I would like to get the name of "foo" from the shell.
I can use ls to do it, but I would like to know if there's a way that is more efficient or less error-prone.

A:

The following command will return the name of the file you're running in the background:
screen -r | grep -E '^\([0-9]*\)$'

If it doesn't work as you expect then try:
cat filename.sh | grep --color=always "^\([0-9]*\)$" | head -n 1

The first one is faster and less prone to errors than using ls.

A:

The syntax for a pointer to a member of the class C is C::*
So your first syntax is correct, but it's also wrong because it doesn’t actually call the function method.  You’d have to use:
p->method();

The easiest way is to open it with an editor and see whether you get the same results as when you double clicked it.
If that doesn't work then you could open it with a hex editor and see if you get a hex dump of a file that doesn't match any of the ones that are encrypted with the same algorithm as the one that you double-clicked.

<|endoftext|>Q:

How to make a list of a certain number with the most common elements of the other lists in Python 3?

Suppose that I have the list of lists,
lst = [[1], [2, 3], [3, 4]]

Now I want to create a list, which contains the number 2 and 4, the number 3 and 4 and all elements that occurs more frequently than the number of elements of the lst. So the result of this should be 
[2, 4, 3, 4].

How should I make this list using Python 3?

A:

You can use the itertools module.
lst = [[1], [2, 3], [3, 4]]

from itert import iz as iz1

lst = [iz1(k)[2] for k in lst]

# [2 4 3 4]

Or, if you prefer, use the sorted(lst) method.
sorted(lst, key=lambda x: x[1])

# [2 4 3 4].

int i=1;

}

This is the code for scanf and fscan. The program works fine if there's only one space in the input but when there's a space between two words, it just prints the first one, ignoring the second.

A:

You're comparing a to EOF, so your program will never terminate if the input string has multiple spaces in it.
You can fix it by checking the result of scan, not the input itself, like this:
int i = 0;
while (fscan (stdin, a[++i], 1) != EOI);

The cache is usually organized to store a copy of data which was recently used by the processor, but is not used again for a while, and the copy may be stored at a lower level in the cache.
The cache can be a small memory in a processor chip or it may be a large cache, such an ECC-cache, which is a cache that has been designed to hold a very long list of valid addresses and is capable of handling multiple address spaces, each having its own unique address space.

<|endoftext|>Q:

Why are some people able to do the following in C?

int i = 10;

printf("\n%d", i + 5);

Output:

15

But why are some people not able to do it? Why are they not able to add the value 5 to the value 10?

A:

The + operator has higher priority than the assignment operator (=). 
The expression 10 + 5 will evaluate the value of 10 before the value of 5, and then it will assign the value 5 to i.
The expression i + 5 will evaluate the value of i before the value of 5, and then it will assign the value 10 to i.

2. Discussion of the Related Art
As is well known, integrated circuits (ICs) may be fabricated by a series of steps which result in the fabrication of one or more semiconductor components, or chips. These steps generally include the deposition of a number of layers on the surface of a substrate.
A layer of insulator is deposited onto the substrate and then a number of metal layers are sequencially deposited over the insulator, with each metal deposited over the insulator being separated by the insulator. This sequence is repeated until a chip is formed.
The metal layers may include a gate electrode layer which is typically composed of polysilicon, although metal layers of other conductivity types such as metals and refractory materials such as tungsten and molybdenum may also be used as the gate electrode layer of the integrated circuits.

<|endoftext|>The invention relates generally to the field of semiconductor devices. More particularly, the invention relates to an improved method of making an electrostatic discharge protective structure for integrated circuits.
As integrated semiconductor devices have become progressively more dense, the amount and rate of current flow between adjacent devices have been reduced. As the amount of current flowing between devices has become less than a specified level, an electrostatic discharge (ESD) condition may occur, which can damage the devices. An ESD event is caused by a human body contacting an integrated circuit (IC) and can cause the IC to be destroyed or damaged.
One approach to protecting integrated circuits has been to provide a conductive structure that extends over the entire surface of a semiconductor chip. This structure is referred to as being a conductive path (CP). The use of such conductive structures has been shown to be useful in reducing ESD stress to the device and protecting the device from ESD.

<|endoftext|>A new approach to the management of severe sepsis and septic shock.
The management of patients presenting with septic shock has been a topic of considerable controversy in the medical literature. A number of studies have demonstrated a mortality rate approaching 30%, with no consensus on optimal therapy. In the absence of randomized clinical trials, the management of septic shock is often guided by extrapolations from studies of other disease processes or by extrapolated data obtained in animal models of sepsis. The most important factors influencing mortality are: (i) severity of seeding; (b) degree of hypotension; and/or (c) presence of a source of infection. The most important factors influencing mortality are: (1) the degree of hypotension, (2) source of infection, and (3) source of shock, although the latter may be secondary to hypotension. The most important determinants of the outcome in patients presenting in septic shock include: source of septic shock (primary versus secondary), degree of hypotension, source of infection, severity of shock (severe vs moderate/mild), and presence of coagulation abnormalities. The goal of therapy is to achieve the best hemodynamic response, which is dependent on the underlying disease, to optimize

This has been demonstrated to occur both by the process of cell migration in a 3-dimensional environment and in vitro by the process of cell invasion through an artificial extracellular matrix. The process of cell migration is dependent upon the local extracellular matrix and the adhesion of individual tumour cells and is influenced by the local extracellular matrix. This is not the case for cell invasion through an artificial extracellular matrix, although cell invasion may be influenced by the adhesion to this matrix. It is suggested that the process of invasion through the extracellular matrix is dependent upon the presence of a local environment which is rich in growth factors which stimulate cell migration and invasion. <|endoftext|>Q:

How to get rid of a specific character after a certain amount of characters?

I have an email address, which is like the following:
john.smith@example.org
I want to remove the '.' from the email address, and then add a '.' after the first two numbers (like john.Smith@example.org).
I've tried the following code:
$address = 'john.Smith@example.org';
echo substr($address, 1, -1); // outputs 'smith'
echo substr($address, 1, strpos($address, '.'));  // outputs 'smith.'

But it outputs 'smith.'. How can I remove '.' and then add a '.' to the address?
Thanks.

A:

$address = 'john.Smith@example.com';
echo substr($address, strpos($address , '.')); // outputs 'smith@example.com'

strpos() returns the position of the string.
substr() takes a string as the first parameter, and removes everything from the first character to the last. So the second parameter is just a string with '.' appended to 'smith'.

How does the compiler know that the type is not a pointer?

When I try the following code
struct A  *a = new A;

a = &i;

but
void func (int a)  //a = &i; is not allowed

When you use a variable name that doesn't exist at all, you are using the name for a different thing.  The name "int" isn't a name that exists, and therefore it is not valid to use it to refer to an integer.
In the second example, you're trying to refer to a non-existent variable called i.  The name i isn't valid to refer to an object.

Why does the compiler warn that I can't convert from int to char[3]?

I was trying to compile the following code with gcc. The following warnings are being shown:

warning: conversion to 'char [3]' from 'int' may change the sign of the result 

Why is the compiler warning me?
#include<iostream>
#include<stdio>
int main(){
 char arr[5];
 for(int i(0,5-1);i!=0 && i!=5;i--)
 arr[i]=0;
}

A:

The conversion from int to char[3] is invalid, but it doesn't mean that you can't use the result as you wish.
You can use it as you wish by casting it to a char array:
char arr[] = {0};

The compiler is warning you that the conversion from int to char may change the sign of the result of the conversion from int to a char.

As is well known in semiconductors manufacturing art, it is a well established procedure to perform a photolithographic technique for fabricating an integrated circuit (IC). More particularly, a photolithographic technique involves forming various patterns of a mask, exposing the mask to a light to form a corresponding image, then etching the underlying layer to transfer the corresponding image to the underlying layer. In this manner, a circuit pattern is formed in the underlying layer to be patterned by a mask, and the circuit pattern thus formed is utilized as the pattern to be formed in a subsequent layer of the underlying layer by etching.
A typical photolithographic technique includes forming a pattern in a photoresist, exposing the photolithographic mask to light through a photo mask, and performing an etches to transfer the pattern to the underlying layer.
