<|endoftext|>A novel approach to improve the accuracy of a three-dimensional finite element simulation of a human femoropubic junction using a finite-element-based patient-specific model. An experimental case report.
The three-dimensional finite element (FE) simulation of human knee joint is one of the main methods for the biomechanics study of knee diseases and injuries. A finite-element-based three-dimensional human femoropubic junction (FPJ) simulation model is a prerequisite to the analysis of the knee joint. This study aims to improve accuracy of a FE simulation of the FPJ model. A novel FE simulation approach was developed and validated with the three-dimensional FE simulation of the FPJ of one human volunteer using the patient-specific model. In the proposed approach, a patient-specific model was constructed based on the three- dimensional FE analysis. A new FE simulation was performed to compare the results. The validation of the proposed approach was successful. The error of FE simulation was reduced from 1.6% to 2.2% in the anterior-posterior and 3D-axial plane. The first is a simple past. The second is a present progressive.

I'm a little (bit) drunk.    
(I've been) drinking.

I've been (not) drinking.

<|endoftext|>Q:

Is there any method of finding a solution to $f(x) = x$?

Is there any method of finding a solution to $f(1) = 1$?
If yes, please provide some method.
If it's not possible, then please explain why it's so.

A:

If $f(a) = a$, then $f(f(a)) = f(a)$ by the Associative Law and $f(a + b) = f(a) + b$ by the Associative Law. 
So $a$ is the only solution to $x = 1$ if $f(a) = a$.

To the extent to the amount of the amount of data

  <user name="username">

    <providers>
      <clear/>

<|endoftext|>Q:

What is the difference in using "for (i = 0; i < 10; i++)" vs. "for (;;)" in this code snippet?

I was looking at the following code snippet:
for (i = 0; i < 10; i++){
    printf("%s\0",i);
}

and the following code snippet:
for(;;){
    printf("%s\0",i);
}

I am curious about the difference between them.

A:

In the first example, the for(;;) block is empty. The code inside it is executed only once, at the end of the for-loop.
In the second example, the for loop is empty. The code inside it is executed only once, at the end of the for-loop.
The first example has a higher readability because you don't need to know that i is 0, 1, ..., 9 at the beginning.
The second example has a higher readability because it doesn't have to be clear what i is at that time.

<|endoftext|>A novel mutation, c.1236G-->A, in the gene encoding the protein-glutamine-rich domain of protein S is associated with familial combined hypercholesterolemia.
Protein S (PRS) is a vitamin K-dependent protein synthesized in the liver. It is a major plasma inhibitor of blood coagulation and a potent inhibitor of angiogenesis. Protein S deficiency causes a severe bleeding tendency due to the inability of the protein to bind factor VIII and von Willebrand factor. In addition, protein S deficiency has also been linked to increased cardiovascular disease risk. In the present study we identified a novel heterozygous mutation (p.Cys412Gly) in the protein-glutamine-rich domain of protein S (PRS) gene (PRSG1), which was detected in three unrelated patients with familial combined hypercholesterolemia. We also performed functional analysis of the protein and the results revealed that the mutation resulted in a significant decrease of the protein expression. Our results suggest that the PRSG1 mutation might be associated with the development of familial combined hypercholesterolemia, and the PRS protein might play a significant role in the regulation of blood coagulation and vascular homeostasis.

How to make the following function recursive

I am trying to write the following recursive function but it is not recursive.
function recursive(n) 
   if n = 0 or n is an even number
   then return n
   if n = 1 or n is an odd number
   then return recursive(n-1)
   else
       recursive(n-1) + 1

A:

The function returns n when it is the only value in the recursion.  The function returns recursive(n-1) when there are other non-0 values in the recursion, which means that the function is recursive when n is an even number and recursive(n-1) is an odd number.  You can see that by adding the two lines that return recursive(n-1) + 1 to the function definition. Titles should not include a list of related topics.

How is the 'in' keyword used in python to check if a value is a list or not?

A:

The in keyword is an operator, not a type.

In this case, it checks whether list is an instance of the type list or not.

<|endoftext|>Q:

Is it possible to make the following code more efficient?
[1,2,3].map(function(elem){
    return (elem+1);
});

This code does the job, but is it possible to improve it further?

A:

You can do this with the reduce method:
[1,2,3].map(function(elem){
    return (elem+1);
}.reduce(function(prev, curr) {
    return prev + curr;
}));

This will give you a new array of all the results, which is the result of the previous function applied to the previous array.

<|endoftext|>Q:

Is the "fractional" notation in the following expression valid?

I have seen the following notation in an equation, but I am not sure if it is valid: 
$$x = \frac{1}{2}(x - 1)^2.$$

A:

It's not valid, because $x - 1 \neq x$ and $2x$ does not have a fractional part. 
You can use the notation $x = \frac12(x-\frac12)^2$ or $x = \frac12(x-\frac12)(x-1)$.

What's the difference between "a" and "the"?

2) The best friend I have. I'm the best friend.

In your first example, the is used as an adjective, and the is a possessive.  In your second example, the is an adjective, and it's the possessive.

<|endoftext|>Q:

How to get a list of all the users who have access to a folder on my computer?

I have a list of all the users who have access to a folder on my computer. I am able to get a list of users with this command:
Get-ChildItem -Path C:\Users\* -Include *.lnk

How would I be able to get all the users with access to a folder and have them listed in a table in a text file?

A:

You could use the Get-Acl command:
Get-Content c:\users.txt | ForEach-Object { $_.GetAcl }

You will probably have to use the -Include parameter to get a list, not an array, of the users with access.  You could use a ForEach-Object to create a new file and then pipe that content to the Get-Content command.
Get-Content c:\test | ForEach-Object {
    New-Item -ItemType File -Item $($_.FullName).txt
    Get-Content c:\users.txt | ForEach-Object { $_.GetAcl }
}

How to get a list of all files with the extension .html?

find / -name '*.html'

find / -name '*.html' -print0 | grep -q -e "\.html$"

<|endoftext|>Q:

What is the difference between these two queries?

I have these 2 queries:
SELECT * FROM users

SELECT * FROM users WHERE email_verified = true

I am wondering which one is the best and which one should I use?

A: If you're looking for the fastest way to do it you should use the second one.
But if you are looking to optimize your application and the queries are similar and you don't want to use indexes you should use the first query because it has the index on users.email_verified.
Also you could do:
SELECT * FROM users
WHERE email_verified = true
LIMITS 1;

And you would be faster than doing the second query because it would only need to check the first row.

<|endoftext|>Q:

What does "invalid argument" mean in this case?

I'm reading a C++ book. The book has a function like this:
void print_array_of_int() {
    int arr[100];
    for ( int i = 0; i < 100; i ++ ) {
       arr[ i ] = i+1; /* invalid argument*/ 
   }
     for ( int i = 1; i <= 99; i ++ ) {
        cout << arr[ i ] << endl;
    };
}

I understand that the function can't handle the array with size greater than 100. But I don't understand what "invalid argument" means in this case.

A:

It means arr is an invalid pointer. 
In the first iteration, it tries to access arr[99], which is invalid (the value is undefined).
In the second iteration, it tries to access arr[100], which is also invalid.
In the third iteration, it tries to access arr[0], which is valid but undefined.

<|endoftext|>Q:

How to use a custom font on the android studio editor

I'm using the android studio editor to edit some text, and I want to use a custom font.
Is there a way to use a custom font? If I create a new text style, and then add a custom font, will the editor recognize it? Or do I have to create a font file in my project, and then add a custom font to it in my text style?

A:

There are two different ways to set a custom font for the editor:

Add custom fonts to the editor. To do that, go to the Fonts menu in your editor and select the font you want to use. Then, click the Add to Text Style button to add it to your text style.
Use the Fonts & Colors menu in the editor. In this case, the font will be used for the whole application.

A third way is to create your own custom font in Eclipse (using the Fonts menu), then use the Text Styles and Fonts options (in the Text Editors menu) to set the font.

A:

def time_difference(t1, t2):

How can I use the same variable to define a function in both a macro and function?

I have a macro that I would like to define in a function and then pass the macro as the first argument of a function that I would like to call. 
Here's the code:
%macro foo(a=1)
%do %while %eval(&a); %end; %end
%mend

%define foo(a,b) %foo(&a,b)

%func bar()

%foo(a=1) bar();

The error I get when I do this is

"foo(a,b)"

The macro foo is called from a macro that I would like to be able to use as a function.

A:

The %eval command is not a function call.  It's just a command that evaluates a string.  It can be used in place of a function call.  You can't use it in the middle of a %macro, though.  You can't use it inside a function, either; it doesn't work in functions.

A. Run-Time Dynamic Huffman Coding
In the run-time dynamic (RTD) Huffman coding technique, each symbol to be encoded is compared with each possible symbol in a symbol table and a symbol is encoded only if the symbol table entry for the symbol is identical to the symbol to be encoded. The symbol table is typically a two level tree. The first level is a symbol table for the most common symbols used in the encoding, and the second level is for the remaining symbols that are not in the table of the most common symbols.

<|endoftext|>A new study has concluded that people who are obese are at greater risk of developing type 2 diabetes, a condition that is often linked by the presence of the hormone insulin.

The study, by researchers at the Mayo Clinic, also showed that obese patients who had the lowest insulin levels were the least likely to develop the disease.

"This is a big step forward, but the question remains as to whether we will be seeing more of these results in the future," said Dr. Mark Blagosklonny.

In the study, published in the journal Obesity, researchers analyzed data on more than 2,000 obese and lean people who were followed for an average of three years.

They found that obese people who had insulin levels lower than those of normal-weight people who had the same insulin levels were much less likely to go on to develop diabetes.

The results are important because they show that a healthy diet and lifestyle can help prevent type 2 diabetes, Blagosklonny said.

"This study shows that a healthy diet can help prevent diabetes in people who are obese," he said.

The study is the first of its kind, and the findings are based on the analysis of a relatively small number of people.

<|endoftext|>QR codes are the universal language of business, used by all kinds of organizations. The QR codes on our cards, on our phone‘, our website, our app are the same thing. QR codes are also called two dimensional bar coding and the most widely used one. They were created by IBM to solve their data collection challenge, where data entry was the biggest bottleneck.
Qr codes are a kind or symbology used to mark information in two dimensions, with the information in the code represented in two colors and black on white or white only on white, and the code being readable by any device, such a smartphone. They also have been widely applied in the field where it can be difficult, or expensive to scan or scan and decode data on an optical character reader (OCX).
The QR Code, as it stands today, uses a standard of two color coding. The color of black or white represents one symbol and a color other symbol, which represents a second symbol in two dimensions and the two symbols can be combined in many ways and can also form a pattern of two symbols and their combinations. The aim here of our investigation of bronchiolus is not to compare the efficiency of different suctation devices but only a preliminary study of a possible difference in effectiveness in the diagnosis and treatment between high speed and high volume broncoscopic suctions in our hospital during a one week time span, using bronchoscope as an objective tool in order of preference. <|endoftext|>The present Invention relates a system and method to control the temperature inside an oven to achieve optimum temperature. It is especially adapted and designed to be utilized in a cooking process. In this process, the oven heats up food in the oven and cooks them by using heat and moisture in an oven chamber, and it also controls the cooking of the heated and moisture food. It is an essential requirement to keep the temperature within an acceptable temperature zone to achieve optimum temperature. The present oven control is capable of maintaining a desired temperature within a temperature zone by adjusting an air supply rate of heat. The temperature is adjusted in a manner of increasing heat supply rate when the heat in an inside chamber reaches a temperature zone to achieve a higher cooking temperature and then adjusting it back by lowering the supply air of the heat, so it is a continuous and cyclable temperature control for an optimum cooking process. The present oven has the advantage to maintain the optimum food temperature for an efficient cooking process. The system is able not only control the air flow of a heating unit to achieve the required heat for cooking process but also to control and control temperature of food in oven. The system has an automatic shut out system that shuts out air from the heating chamber if the temperature in an oven chamber goes above its temperature zone and

<|endoftext|>A novel approach for assessing aortoiliac graft flow. Aortofascoplasty. A new technique.
This is the second article to report a new procedure designed for evaluating flow characteristics of anastomosing vessels. The technique, termed the "augmentation flow technique", consists primarily or entirely of the use, in an arteriogram obtained during aortofemoral bypass operation, or other procedure requiring arterial graft flow measurement, or during an angioscopic study or angiocardiographic investigation in aorta-narrow-grafted vessels or the abdominal aorstomy stump, and the subsequent injection and observation with the arterial injection port or the injection needle, respectively; and in this way a continuous measurement can also made in other arterial vessels. It has the following major advantages compared in the previous report: it is more rapid than conventional techniques; more reliable, and easier to use than previous techniques and, therefore less traumatic for a patient, because it avoids any possible injury or damage caused during a conventional measurement of flow in the anastomal area; and more easily applied to all patients, because it does not involve the need or the time required to prepare the vessel for the examination or to expose the injection or

<|endoftext|>A new species from New Calhonia, New South wales
The new species, C. kapuae is the second member of C. katangana, and was collected on a rocky beach, about 1 km south east of New Heavies.
It can easily be identified on the basis of its unique pattern of black-brown colouring. Cephalic and labral areas have black and brown spots, with a black line between each spot; there also appears to be a series on either flank that extends into a black line, with two or perhaps 3 spots in each row on either flank and a series of spots between these rows, with 2 or maybe 3 black dots on each spot. This pattern of black, brown spots with black and white lines is a unique character of its kind and has been found nowhere in Australia. The new species can readily be separated into three colour morphos (or groups):
Group A: black spots with black line
(a) Black with a line of black between the two black dots
b) Brown spots, one spot between each row, and black between rows, with a series on one of two flank, with a black band at midlength and two dots on a spot in each series

Embody computing also includes software systems or hardware devices whose functionality is embedded into a larger software or computing system to provide an improved, expanded and/in some cases, novel computing function. A patient monitor system was implemented using QR Codes to communicate the status or status information about a patient to the hospital personnel or to the medical personnel outside. A prototype patient monitor system was used as an experiment for patient care in a general medicine unit at a medical centre in Singapore, with two medical specialists and a medical technician using QR Codes in a patient room. The medical personnel in a ward room and in other wards could monitor and control patient status information by entering a specific QR Code into their smartphones. A prototype QR Code-aided monitoring/controlling (PCMS) was used in an intensive-care unit to improve medical staff's awareness about the patient. Why does the "new" operator in python not work?

The following code:
def f():
    print "Hello, World!"

x=f()

print "x: "

y=f()

print "y: "

z=f()

print x,
print y,
print z

output:
x: Hello,World
y: Hello,World
z: None

Why does the "x" variable not have a name in "f"?

A:

You are using an object as the variable name. 
x = f() is a function call, and you're not assigning a name (x) to the function.
You're creating an instance of an object with a name (z):
z = f()

You're also creating an object with a name (f) that has been assigned to the function object (x) (which is what the function call returns).
The name f is only created in the context of the function f, and is not available outside of it, so it cannot be used as the object name for any other function.

How is this function differentiable?

Let $f(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^2}}$ be a continuous function on $(0, \infty)$. Then $f$ is differentiable.

A:

Let $\epsilon > 0$ be any positive real number.
By the fundamental theorem of calculus, there exists a real number $t$ such that
$$\frac{f'(x)}{f(x)} - \frac{1}{x} \leq \epsilon \quad \text{ for all } x \geq t$$

What are the benefits of using the new "async" syntax? (or is there any benefit in the new syntax?)

What are the benefits of using the new "asynchronous" syntax in javascript? (or is there any advantage in the new syntax)

I'm using the "asynchronous" syntax to avoid the "callback()" syntax and the "async()" method. I'm not sure if I should use the new syntax.

A:

There is not much benefit in the new syntax. The new version is not a new feature.  The "asynchronous" part is an implementation of the "as" keyword.
If you have a function that uses the new syntax, and then you want a function to be called with "async" and "callback", the "async" keyword is not needed.  The syntax you are using is not "asynchronous"; it has the syntax for calling a callback function. The syntax that you are using for "async" does not have any meaning, except as a way to create an object that is used as a callback function.

<|endoftext|>Q:

How to get the name of the file that is being processed?
I have a file with a bunch of lines that looks like:
1
2
3
4,5
6

How can I extract the name of the file that is being processed?
I am using a program called fgrep which is written in Perl, but that is not relevant to my problem.
Thanks in advance.

A:

You can use the -f option to the grep. 
$ grep -f 'file' file.txt

This will return only the file name in the first field of the output. 
If you want to print the whole file name you can use the -o option. It will print all the fields in the output.
$ grep -o "file\|\n" file.txt

This will output:

  file
/home/jim/file2.txt

<|endoftext|>The effector function of CD4 T cells in the induction and maintenance of tolerance is dependent on CD4 TCR-mediated activation of NFAT.
We have investigated whether CD4 TCR activation of the transcription factor NFAT is required for T cell tolerance to self-antigen. We have previously shown in mice deficient in CD4 TCR, that CD4 TCR-dependent induction of NFAT is required for the development of tolerance to self-antigen. Yes you can. 
In the declaration you can use a reference.

class MyClass {

  int method1(int a, int b);
  ...
}

MyClass obj; 
MyClass &obj_ref = obj;

How is the value of an integer in a variable stored in a variable in Python?

>>> x = 1
>>> y = 2
>>> print x, y
1, 2
>>> print y, x
1, 2
>>> print y, x
1, 2
>>>

You can also use the print() built-in function, which prints the current value of the variable.
>>> print(y, x = 2)
1
>>> x, y = 2
>>> print(y, x)
1

You could use the assignment operator to make it so that x = y is equivalent to x = y + 1.

<|endoftext|>A new approach for the detection of a single-base mutation in a short-read DNA sequence.
The detection of point mutations in DNA sequence has been one of the most difficult problems in the molecular analysis of the genome, and the current methods for this task are not satisfactory. In this paper, we present a new method based on a simple and efficient approach for the detection of single-base mutations in a short-read DNA sequence. The approach is called the mutation-detected-sequence (MDS) method, and it can detect mutations in the range from a single base to several hundred base pairs. The method is based on a simple idea that a mutation in a short-read is detected by the change in the frequency distribution of the read length. The MDS method is applied to two sets of DNA sequences, one set consisting of the DNA sequences of the human genome, and the other set consisting of the DNA sequences of a random sequence. It is shown in our experiments that the MDS method can detect single base mutations as well as other types of single base substitutions and single base deletions. The study, "The Impact of the Information-Bias on the Perceived Accuracy of a Story," published in the journal Social Cognitive and Behavioral Sciences, found that when a story is told in a way that is biased toward the fiction, the story's perceived accuracy is lower than a story that is told unbiasedly by presenting the same information in both ways.

Researchers found that bias is the result of two processes: a cognitive process that is driven by how the information is presented and the information itself, and a process driven by how the storyteller chooses to present the information.

We studied Cryptosporidium spp. in the gastrointestinal tract, feces, and feces-susceptible fish from the Pacific Northwest in 2011 (n = 16) and 2012 (n = 23). The fish-associated Cryptosporidium spp. included 14 isolates of C. parvum and two C. meleagridis genotypes previously known to be associated with Pacific Northwest populations, as well as one C. parvum isolate from a fish-susceptible zebrafish captured outside the region and sequenced using Illumina MiSeq, generating 2,500,853 reads. The two C. meleagridis genotypes were closely related and were both found sporadically in two of the two C. meleagridis hosts from Pacific Northwest waters. This represents the first C. meleagridis genotype identified in the Pacific Northwest and the first documented occurrence of C. parvum isolates from Pacific Northwest waters in fish. [Mental preparation needed concerning antiblanking operations; experiences based at Gires area by hospital staff in the 1980's].
After a survey of the medical staff at three local hospitals in the years of 1979-82, the authors present their conclusions concerning preparedness against "nesting" and other forms of aggressive acts or behaviour carried out by patients and relatives as means of controlling their mental processes. Special attention is given to the preparation needed by staff in dealing with patients suspected of aggressive behaviour and in particular, the need to keep in close touch with other staff members and other people, who could offer support, such as the family circle or the social services. The author also points out the importance of an awareness training programme, of which the hospital nurse is a central actor, in preparing people concerned about aggressive acts. The general discussion and the results of the survey show that the hospital staff needs more training in order to prepare against the aggressive acts carried out by patients and their relatives, as well as in regard to the mental preparation needed for dealing with aggressive acts by a staff member.

Diproparginylpropiole for control of inflammation, osteoprosis, atherogenesis and transplant rejection.
Two non-steroid anti-inflammatory drugs, i.e., 1-propargylpropiole and 1,3-dipropargylpropylpropiole, are now in clinical trials in many countries. Dipropargylpropiole is a stable derivative of 1-propargylpropiole which should be much more potent than the original with a substantially lower incidence of gastrointestinal upset. The efficacy of 1-propargylpropiole has been well documented in animal models but the efficacy in humans is only beginning to be characterized. Dipropargylpropiole exhibits anti-inflammatory, analgesic and antipruritic activity in animals when applied topically at higher doses. Dipropargylpropiole also exerts significant inhibition of cyclooxygenase-2, as a result of its selective antagonistic activity of glucocorticoid receptors. Its mechanism of action is unknown but appears to be associated with an effect at the receptor rather than at the transcriptional initiation site in the promoter of cyclooxygenase-2. Nuclear matrix: An enigma unveiled of chromatin conformation by electron paramagnetic resonance and X-ray crystallography.
The nuclear matrix is a nuclear compartment of heterochromatin that consists of heterochromatic nuclear matrix domain-containing proteins (NM-CODIN) as their chromatin-associated constituents. NM-CODIN have been shown to possess chromatin remodelling properties. Here we used electron paramagnetic resonance (EPR) and X-ray crystallography experiments to show that in the absence of transcription, the NM-CODIN form an aggregated protein-protein complex at the nuclear matrix. Electron microscopy and immunofluorescent staining with anti-NM-CODIN and anti-SM-CODIN showed this NM-CODIN complex formation was due to the presence of NM-CODIN with intact binding domains that can self-assemble at the nuclear matrix. To understand the role of nuclear pore complex (NPC) proteins in chromatin-NPC interactions, we investigated Nup153- and Nup62-NPC proteins. In modern high-tech systems (a representative representative herefrom will further designate generally "apparatus") such as computers, communications equipment, and others, the system usually uses a communication device such as a modem, for example, for connection to a network.
The communication device can receive data from a transmitter via a data channel. The data channels are usually connected in parallel, with the number of the data channels in parallel for use in an apparatus being variable. The communication device usually has a number of input and output pins (terminals) such as pins 7 and 10 of FIG. 1. These terminals can be connected to the input/output pins of the data channel of an apparatus. For example, terminal 10 could be connected to the input pin of data channel 11 of an apparatus. Terminal 7 could be connected to the output pin of data channel 11 of the apparatus.
The data channel (or interface) can comprise, for example, a 4.3-volt PNP device as illustrated in the block of FIG. 2. Culture as such does exist without being an exclusive characteristic peculiar with particular members, or a special feature of a particular group. It can be said that culture can be defined as a social and personal attitude or lifestyle which can be expressed in terms of words and images; that which is characteristic, distinctive, or special. Culture is a product which arises from social interaction and individual interests. Culture is usually said to be a characteristic aspect of the individual, which exists both within a person and outside of it through the influence of his or her surroundings, that is, environment.
Culture is generally defined as a product which is formed either by the person, or by the environment or other people within the person’s social environment. Culture in this context means that part of an individual’s attitude which can be expressed through a language, and which is characteristic, distinctive, or special to that person. The product of culture includes both internal and external factors which are influenced by the person’s surroundings. Culture usually has the form of a personal philosophy, values and behaviour system, and so can be considered to be an expression of oneself. Culture can be viewed as an aspect of personality and personality as an aspect of culture.

[Ebend-cored-tube, autoplacentering or other prosthetic endourethral stent for treatment of post traumatic stress incontinence in women after radical retropubic prostatectomy].
To report outcomes of different endourethral prosthetic techniques in women after radical retropubic prostatectomy. All women who had a radical retropubic prostatectomy from November 2009 to December 2013 were included in a retrospective study. We recorded patient demographics, intravesical instillation and duration, postoperative complications, follow up and recurrence rate. A total of 100 cases were included in the study. But what does one's own "business" mean? I think it is a misused phrase and it is often used in a way that conveys, at best, only a vague idea of what the speaker wishes to convey and, at worst, the speaker does not even know what the phrase is referring to.
Let's look at some examples of this and attempt to clarify the meaning using the dictionary definitions.

1) The business of one's own (and family etc.,) is whatever one's "own business" (my) is.

This definition conveys only a vague idea, the reader might or might not guess what the next sentence is about.

2) The "business" of (the country) as we know it is its people, businesses and institutions.

Again, this is a vague idea: is it the business of one's country, the business of the nation or the business of the people?  The same is the case if we say the business of a nation is its people, businesses and institutions.

Tunnels & Dung is not sponsored anymore: It started some 8 year before we existed at this blog. I’m writing about what we’ve come to know as the tunnel network in the city we’ve loved.

It’s been a slow process to let Tunnels & Dung be a blog about the stuff we love about our city. Here’s how we’ve changed it:

This blog started because we had some friends in our community that could not afford a home because their landlord was a real estate mogul and had money. We learned that our friend’s landlord had a great reputation for building quality and environmentally friendly homes built on the side of the road (with no street access). So we started talking to him and eventually met him and his friend at a local farmers market. It turned out he had a good feeling about our friend’s house because he had seen lots of people drive through the front yard and leave with bags of produce, fruits, and veggies. So he gave our friend’s friends $100 down payment. In exchange, his friend agreed to allow the house to be built on the empty lot so they would not need a lot for a house.

Jak/Myelinated (J/M) is a family of proteins that can cause the formation of a series of immunological signal transducer protein phosphorylated by receptor kinases and is involved in the transmission of nerve impulse signals to effectors such as the cytoskeletal proteins. The drug development of the family members requires a thorough understanding of both the protein structures, the binding interaction with tyrosine kinase, and the effects of drug action on the function of the intracellular signal transducers. A family member, inhibit J/M (Sigma J/M), is a highly potent inhibitor with a selective binding and inhibitory activity for the receptor tyrosine kinase Tyro3,3'Dio3. Sigma J/M is an anti-infection drug candidate with a good therapeutic potential that inhibits the proliferation of the cell line Hep3B. This specification provides systems level integration features that leverage an operating network in combination the Ethernet protocol. More specifically, the network may be used for access control, communication and multimedia. The system integration technology described herein may be applied to a wide range of operating networks, but it is generally applicable to ATM (asynchronous transfer mode) networks for voice and data transport.
The system integration technology described herein is particularly useful when applied to ATM networks. In a typical network using the ATM technology, a number of end nodes are interconnected using an ATM network. The network functions using the Internet Group Management Protocol, or IGMP, and an Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) server. The network manages traffic in the network according to the IGMP. This traffic is divided into “packets”. Typically each “packet” consists of a header followed by one or more ATM cells. In a single packet, the header carries management information such as an IP address of a particular source interface, a destination interface, and routing information used to construct a virtual circuit from source to destination.
Typically each “packet” is transported on a packet-by-packet basis. Packets that are destined to the same destination interface are put in the same virtual

A total of 604 measurements for 16 cephalometric parameters with 1 mm step of increase between values, were compared for intra-individually reliability (intraclass correlation coefficient), intra-individual variation (variation coefficient) and reliability over periods based on dental measurements, using intra-individual measurements and dental measurements. As far as possible, the same method was used for comparison of cephalometric parameters over periods based on dental measurements and with different measurement techniques. The evaluation of cephalometric radiographs on panoramic radiographs was not influenced by the measurement technique used. When the measurements with the cephalometric radiographs were compared with the measurements with the panoramic radiograph, the intra-individual variation was less than that with the dental measurement technique. Dose Escargue-Nembutirano-Pariento/Cordova

Dose Escargue-Nembutirano-Pariento (E.S. - Nembutirano / Cordova) is an independent news media, founded in 1996, which currently has two websites and two radio programs distributed on various radio stations and podcasts on iTunes, YouTube, Google Play, and Spotify.
As of May 2019, its website provides news, politics, human-interest stories, culture, entertainment, weather and general information. The site was originally focused on the city of Cordova and has changed its focus since 2012. For over a decade the site also included international news and global news. In 2012, it became more focused on Mexico while still sharing stories with more international news. In late 2012, Dose Escargue's website was taken down by the Mexican internet providers for being too popular.

Radio program
Its two flagship radio programs are Dose Escargue México and Hora de la Patria. Dose Escargue México covers the news from all of the major Mexican cities across the country. It focuses on different social, cultural, and political issues of the various Mexican cities.

Anomalisms and congenitances found before surgery-case example: the case of HN1-Bickel syndrome on the right side.
HN1-Bickel syndrome is a rare multisystemic, autosomal dominant, lethal, congenital, intrauterine multiform, hereditary, and progressive myopathy, the main symptoms being muscle weakness and myotonia of all tissues, particularly of respiratory muscles. This condition is characterized by an extremely variable clinical expression: from severe to mild to moderately mild to severe; from complete to partial to mild, from late-to-rapid to rapid. In the most severe cases are also some congenital abnormalities, such as cardiac defects, dysmorphic facial features, micrognathia, tracheoesophageal fistulas (TEEF), hypertelorism (with or without down-slanting palpebral fissures), and microcrania. The condition has become more and more frequent as the knowledge of this rare disease has increased and with the discovery of gene mutations. There are several types characterized by different gene mutations, but we report on type 3 HN1-Bickel syndrome, the most severe, with mutations involving the X-linked locus

Hang your banner! The best place left? A question most players don't give as much thought as they should. There are a variety of different places around town to hang your banner. As a general rule, hanging the banner on a tree near the top is recommended.



Hanging a banner is a time-consuming and labor-intensive process. Once you have hung your banner, you will find little in the way of a reward. There are no medals/purchasing power/champions/trophies for hanging the banner and you should not count your banner as your badge.



Once you have hung your banner, your banner sits at the top of the tree for 24 hours and then goes to waste. You will only receive 2 more attempts to be able to hang your banner before it disappears.



The time it takes to hang your banner is determined by two things: the weight in your banner and the wind. The higher the weight and the stronger the wind (less than 5 miles per hour), the longer it takes to hang your banner. If you have a very low-weight banner and have a very strong wind, then your banner may hang for hours!

NASHWACO PIZONAS IS BACK…

On Thursday September 4, 2014, the community of Nashwaco were able to attend their annual pizza making demonstrations at The Old Westbury Pizza. We had asked many to attend this year and all agreed and we were able to squeeze into the old Pizza Hut building and had a great opportunity to see the process of building pizza. It is always fun to watch and it is also educational to see how each step of the process works. While everyone in attendance had a great time, the event still would not have been possible without the help and generosity of a few people who donated money to our cause. Our goal was $3,500 which was done and a check was left with the Pizza Shack. If anyone would have just left a few dollars we could have had a much better pizza party and also made the event a much more fun show of appreciation for our donation. Thank you to Don Linn and Ron Ketchum for helping us on the day. We are extremely humbled and grateful and would not have been able to make it without you. It was a good day for everyone and we hope the community will hold you in mind during the coming years.

QTL mapping with single plant-parent intractacies: two model methods? A comparison of mapping models, linkage mapping with pedigree and GBS techniques.
Genetic and phenotypic data of multiple individuals are usually analysed with a model-based association method. These methods may show either two models of inheritance, a model of two additive effects or a model of three additive effects. This paper compares the ability to capture the genetic variation of two different methods - linkage mapping with parental pedigrees using a segregation ratio and GBS. The linkage mapping method was implemented on the whole genome by using the Iberian bean phytonut 60K SNP markers, considering its higher density. The phenotyping of the lines was performed with eight agronomic traits. The GBS was performed on genomic DNA of the lines to obtain a high density SNP map of the lines. The results for the association mapping for the two models of two additive effects in the Iberian bean and the three additive effects in the soybean show a good ability to capture the genetic variation of the lines, despite the higher density for soybean. This means that a simpler association mapping will be achieved, despite having a higher density map in soybean. The results for the two models of inheritance showed

[Biodivtacation characteristics as characteristics parameters to investigate of ecological pollution: review].
Biodivtacation characteristics are characteristic parameters of living organisms and ecosystems. Biodivtacation characteristics can reflect the life trends and development characteristics of environment. This paper mainly reviews the biodivtacation characteristics of the ecosystem, and the methods to get the biodivtacation characteristics. The author selected bivalve fish or invertebrates as the organisms for study, then analyzed and reviewed some biodivtacation characteristics of the ecosystem, and finally proposed some parameters to detect and evaluate the ecological pollution. The paper may be beneficial to the development of new biodivtacation characteristics.

Bidar-Laghdais Road to be Dedicated as an International Border Crossing By 2020

The government on Tuesday announced its decision to award a Rs 2,000-crore contract for building an International Border Crossings at the Bidar-Lagdais Border to a consortium, led by India’s global construction company Larsen and Toubro Limited. They, however, have not revealed the names of their partners.

The project is a part of Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s ambitious agenda to develop the country’s economy by upgrading infrastructure around the country’s key gateway, Delhi. The plan to build an international border crossing between the two National Capital Region is likely to be completed within a span of six to seven years and would enhance the security situation in the border areas between Maharashtra and Uttar Pradesh.

The proposed international border crossing would comprise a two-lane bridge and three lanes for vehicular traffic, with the bridge capable of handling 80,000-100,000 tonnes of traffic per day. The road would be located 10.5 km north of a National Highways project, connecting the National

Komarnytshivtze–Livnoe Highway

Komarnytshivtze–Livnoe Highway is a major highway network in eastern Ukraine, connecting Komarnytshivtze to Livno via a highway junction in the area of Pervomaisk, where it begins.

The road starts in the city of Komarnytshivtze, a historic monument and regional state administrative center in Khersonsk Raion of Zaporizhia Oblast.

Originally this highway ran from here to the western direction, passing through the city of Pervomaisk. The highway was built in the mid-20th century, when the Kherson-Pervomaisk road was modernized by the Soviet state.

This first intercontinental workshop will take place November 5-8, 2019, at the University of Applied Sciences Küsnacht in a building of the Institute of Materials and Composites, University Linz (Linz, Austria).

It was agreed that the venue would be the University of Applied Sciences Küsnacht in the city of Altenburg (Austria), where experts from the world of materials science meet up to explore possibilities of improving mechanical and electric properties of polymers in a synergistic manner.

This workshop aims to address the following questions:

Which are the opportunities where chemical modifications of carbon nanotubes (CNTs) and graphene are the most promising? With which polymeric matrices are nanostructures applied

[New drug and new therapies to overcome the current "golden age"; problems of a long list of potential therapeutic actions and the future for pharmacotherapy].
The new therapeutic strategies were evaluated in a review. The article discusses the new therapeutic opportunities concerning the current "golden age" of antirheumatic drugs in RA, which could be the focus of further research to treat these patients. Also discussed are problems that are associated with any of the listed options. A major goal for future research should be to create a broad spectrum of new therapeutic agents from those currently available. The results of such basic research could then be tested in patients in clinical trials.

Giant retrovesus of Cisnes
Cirrha cistina var. eximius

This species inhabits moist deciduous forests between 900m-2000m, being found in the canopy and also at low level. The tree height ranges from 20-25m, trunk diameters are 70cm-120cm.

The leaf-blotch of this species causes the appearance of whitish spots, mainly located on the uppermost and/or lower areas of the leaves, and are most intensively distributed on the lower leaves, particularly on their lower surfaces, although it can be recognized also on the upper ones. There is a clear pattern of concentric and radial leaf-patterns in this infestation. The blotch does not cause visible damage. The symptoms are most noticeable when the leaves are in the process of unfolding and, in cases of severe infestation, the fruit could be damaged during the blooming stage. This species is most abundant at middle and high elevation (above 1400m). The flowering period runs from late June to mid-July.

Migration History In Context by David Rohsen/i.Times / Pixabay"Migration History in Context" brings together and contextualizes migration histories as they emerge in the history books, news media, and the personal histories of readers at the dawn of modern migration.

"Migration History in Context" brings together and contextualizes migration histories as they emerge in the history books, news media, and the personal histories of readers at the dawn of modern migration. It provides an analytical framework for the history of immigration and migration by considering historical, cultural, and political factors that influence migration and the movement of people during particular periods. It also examines how historical and biographical factors and social-cultural contexts affect migration narratives and representations of individuals and migration movements.

Lateral lary-stopal microdischarge during prolonged larynngization during the first half of swallowing.
The larynx is one of the most complex structures in the human body. A significant percentage of the microdischarges of the larynx during the first half of swallowing (first microdischarge [FMD]) were found to be lateral (inferior and superior to the midposterior commissure). There has been no detailed and systematic examination of them. In this study, all the FMDs during larynngization were investigated in detail and the frequency and characteristics of them were presented. A total of 1,016 swallowing movements in five normal males were examined. FMDs occurred in 25% of all the swallowing movements examined and more than half of them had duration of more than four seconds. The majority of the FMDs were lateral; in 13 of 21 movements, more than half of the FMDs were in lateral position. The main characteristics of the FMDs were the shape (echolalic or inhomogeneous), position (lateral, superior, inferior, or posterior to the midposterior commissure), duration (more than four seconds), and position at the initiation of

Gemcitabine-lipetofeline and FAP regimen for a small cell lung cancer patient with metastases of both the hepatic and the lung origins.
Malignant tumors frequently affect internal organs, and the liver is one of the most common sites reported to be involved in metastasis. We treated a 61-year-old man with a stage IV small cell lung cancer (SCLC) who had multiple liver metastases and distant metastases in both the lung tumors and the peripharyngeal lymph nodes. After the primary disease was surgically eradicated, he underwent radiotherapy and six courses of combination chemotherapy consisting of gemcitabine (GEM), lipetofeline (LIP) and paclitaxel (TAX). Four months after the completion of these chemotherapy, computed tomography (CT) scan revealed that his lung metastases had reduced in size and metastasis had also diminished to a small number of metastatic lesions in the peripharyngeal region. However, his liver metastases showed little change in size and the liver volume was unchanged. After 12 months of the observation period, the liver metastasis of the previous stage remained unchanged and only a few new metastatic lesions appeared in the periparoid

Sara, which was issued in the middle of the Christmas shopping frenzy and the holiday season, is also now the best-selling jazz album since 1998, when Miles Davis & Bill Evans released Kind of Blue at No. 3. According to Nielsen SoundScan, it is moving well beyond expectations.

For the first time since 1958, another jazz album sold more units last weekend during the holiday weekend than jazz albums of other kinds. A comparative case series from Pakistan on complications related after orthognotherapy versus surgery based procedures for myopic and hyperopic astigmatism.
To compare the postoperative management, complications, visual acuity, and refractive astigmatism after surgery versus orthognathic surgery in myopia and hyperopia. The study was done at ENT Hospital in Lahore in an urban setup. A comparative clinical case series was carried out from 1 January 2015 to 31 August 2015. The study was comprised of 40 patients with astigmatism (20 with myopia and 20 with hyperopia) who underwent surgery or ortho-orthodontics. There were 38 male and 2 female patients. All patients had good preoperative alignment. Visual acuity improved after surgery or ortho-orthodontics. Astigmatic errors did not improve after surgery in myopia and hyperopia at any stage. However, the visual acuity of patients was better following surgery or ortho-orthodontics. After surgery or orthodontics, the astigmatic errors did not correct.

The sky above is not white, but the color is deeper, and the sky seems to glow. It reminds me of a time I spent sitting alone in this very area in the late 70’s/early 80’s. I had a little telescope, but it was small and would not stay mounted in the cupola. No one else in my family had this, and because my wife was very active in our church we had not had the occasion to look up at night.

This was not a big deal to me, but of course I spent a lot of time imagining what it may have been like to feel the cold, with the stars as brilliant as a diamond hanging in the night. Lateral root orientation and formation during postroot zone induction. Invivo and ex situ analysis.
Leaflets from 3-year-old Vicia faba L. plantlets were subjected to two or three pre-root treatment stages: 1) pre-induction treatment; and 2) after root induction. Each treatment step resulted in distinct lateral root (LR) phenotypes. In the presence of 1-0-2 root induction medium (1R IM), treatment stage 1 induced short-lateral roots and the formation of new axillary (Ax), intermediate (Int) and primary (Pr) lateral roots in the post-Ind treatment. Treatment 2 was more effective than treatment 1, and resulted in the formation of new Ax, Int and Pr LR phenotypes in the post-Ind treatment. Treatment with both 1R IM and 2R IM induced similar LRs and allowed the formation of new, albeit non-identical Ax, Int and Pr LR phenotypes as well as the formation of a new Ax LR type. The Ax LR phenotype was consistently expressed in the pre-Ind treatment and 1R IM, whereas the Ax LR type was the result of a second, but not first induction event. Intermediate type was found in 1R IM and 2

the use value. Then you'd make your initial calculation:

 __value < total_payment_to_you

That is the basic principle behind the "sophisticated" method of calculating a percentage: you keep track of both sides of the original value, so you don't have to keep track of the final result.
The real issue here is the "complex" method of calculating a percentage. First, you add together the percentage each side gets (e.g. 15% and 25%). Then, you divide one of the resulting numbers by the other. So the "simple" method of determining a percentage is not entirely complex.
It will work for values of money that add up to the sum of both sides of the total payment, but if there is some variance between those sums then the "sophisticated" method of calculating a percentage will work just fine. That is why I said "sophisticated" method, not "complex". If you only expect that the amount would be divisible by $10, then you wouldn't need the complicated method. It would work without it if both sides are divisible by $10, but you have to be careful not to treat them as being divisible by the same number

TENSA OF RÍŠNA I MEZZAPERTUM (14th century) is a painting by Jan Bielančák.

It was bought in 1992 on Slovianka Street in Bratislava by the Bratislava municipality and is now in the Slovak National Gallery of the Slovak National Museum in Bratislava. The Slovak National University of Arts in Bratislava offers a detailed description of the artist's work, including analysis of the work at the same museum; this analysis is accompanied by a detailed illustration of the drawing of an elephant, as well as a video. 

The image is dated to 1467–1469.

In 1993, a reproduction with a facsimile of the artist's work was made for the exhibition in Bratislava at the Slovak National Gallery (MUK), where it was shown in the same year, under the heading of "Tensa of Ríšný Mezzapertum (Slovakia), Slovakia". Aspirapyrins are structurally closely appoxeqmate compounds that, with the exception of those belonging to the acyclic series, have hitherto been synthesized by total synthesis.
Acyclic aspiritapyrrole derivatives and certain other acyclic derivatives have been reported but not the tricyclic derivative. The tricyclic compound has been reported to show an affinity for the dopamine (DA) D-1 and D-2 binding sites in vitro, the affinity of which has been confirmed by radioligand binding experiments. In animal studies, the tricyclic compound has been found to produce psychotomimetic properties, both in animal models and in human volunteers, indicating the potential for abuse in humans and that it may have a high potential abuse liability.
It has not hitherto been possible to produce the tricyclic aspiritapyrrole in sufficient yield to allow such chemical synthesis.

Treatment and pathobidicity for bivalent (antivicrobial, and antineuroprotective) drugs.
The main problems of antimicrobial therapy are resistance of the pathogens, toxicity, and side effects that reduce therapeutic efficacy. With the current increase in resistance to conventional and novel antimicrobial drugs in infectious and immunological diseases, novel and safer antimicrobial drugs are under intensive consideration. The aim of this review paper is to summarize the current evidence on the pathobidicity of bivalent drugs (antivirals and antineuropathic drugs). A search in PubMed, Science Direct, Web of Knowledge, Google scholar, and other databases was carried out using the keywords bivalent, antiviral and antineuropathic drugs. The main issues related to their pathobidicity are the risk of development of antineuroparalytic drug-resistant virus and of cross-reactivity for human receptors, enzymes, and ion channels. There are a few studies on the possible pathobidicity for these substances. However, these compounds are considered safe and well tolerated for use in humans. A survey for drug use knowledge or intention: what does generalizable-based testing yield or miss?
In the search for a model of knowledge of drug use for testing, an investigator is usually faced with different aspects of it or a lack of it. For any model to be a good fit to the data, all of the aspects of it should be a part of the model. We can then find out whether data collected by one investigator are generalizable to another one. Generalizability can then be gauged for a model. This paper reports on our efforts to address a key question--how to improve knowledge for testing in this area in a generalizable way. Although we were primarily concerned with drug use knowledge, our findings are generalized over different aspects of other types of knowledge, for example, knowledge of how medications work, how they are used and how they should be used, or the knowledge that may be necessary to use drugs in a particular way. The results of our work can be summarized in two points: 1) for testing in generalizable-based research, there is a need to find a way to improve knowledge, and 2) there is some room for improvement in the way knowledge is gathered for research purposes. Published Tue 17 Mar 2009

World Rugby wants to take rugby out of the ground, says Steve Borthwick. He thinks rugby is about the world at present - including South Africa and Australia - and should not be played in the ground, with the ball, in Leeds.

Leeds has just changed hands, and that may mean the end of the sport in the ground it has made its home.

Practical advice against "antiallery drug misuse". Effect on patients following a serious adverse drug reaction.
A prospective study of 101 consecutive cases of adverse drug reaction (ADR) referred to an allergy unit is presented. Seventeen cases of life threatening adverse drug reactions were reported. All had been treated with a single course of the suspected drug in the past. The number and severity of symptoms experienced varied considerably between different allergic patients, with no apparent common cause. Most common symptoms were those affecting the respiratory system and the skin. There is evidence (in particular following treatment with a drug used in the emergency room) that most patients may be suffering from a serious adverse drug reaction and should be referred to an allergy unit.

IgSF, a new factor family regulating antibody secretion and secretion kinetics in murine lymphocytes.
IgSF is a member of the Ig supergene family, which is characterized by a conserved three-dimensional domain architecture. These genes usually have one or two Ig V segments, one or two Ig C3 segments, and a constant Ig C2 region, whereas the number of Ig D segments varies and these Ig D-containing members are also called as IgD. It was found that IgD-containing Ig-related (IgDR) factor family (IgSF) are the most abundantly expressed members among the IgSF family, and that they exhibit high levels of expression in many types of cells, including the B lymphocytes and plasma cells. In this review, we describe the IgSF and the role of IgSF in B lymphocyte development, maturation, and development, the maturation and development of B cells, antigen recognition and effector functions in B cells, and B cells, plasma cells, and plasma cells. In conclusion, we demonstrate that the IgSF family, which has diverse expression and roles not only in lymphoid lymphocytes, but also in other cell types, are new factors and genes in immune system development.

Borussia Monuments & Sports Palace Museum (Poland / Гомосарії прикордоння, Góra pokoju na banderę; , GUM)—in the city of Toruń, Torun, Poland. It was opened on May 4, 2007. The museum includes many sculptures made and sculpted by artist August Macke. August Macke has created a number of sculptures, which include a statue of Pope John Paul II. The museum is located in Toruń.

The museum's main entrance is marked by a large iron gate, where a man of a lion sculpture can be seen. Inside the main building, there is a statue of Saint Wojtcz (Saviour), made of iron. The statue stands on a plinth. This is connected to the building's main hall. The hall was built on the foundations of a medieval manor house. The hall was created in 1638 as a stable and barn.

The museum's hall was renovated in 2011.

Exhibitions
Each season offers a separate exhibition. Art objects displayed in the museum are also displayed at Toruń

Pyrrhiun Kulchurs and Hristom Holomortchai of Lithuania

Pyrrhiun Kulchurs and Hristom Holomortchai of Lithuania were two ancient people of the Samogitian ethnic group who fled Lithuania and settled near Dnipro and Kiev in the 16th and 17th century.

Life 
The first account of Hristom Holomortchai dates back to 1612, when the famous historian, Sviatoslav Tsarev mentioned a Lithuanian prince of the family of Holomortchai, Hristo-Moroz, who was married to a "Nerian" princess.

Routine laboratory measurements do provide important opportunities
during emergencies as regards risk assessment and medical decision
making, but these require sophisticated analyses and interpretation.

In
this context, the following routine laboratory tests have proved valuable
for decision making in cases of sepsis.

Hematocrit

The
Hematocrit is an indicator of fluid
retention based on the number of red blood cells in the circulating blood.
A high Hct is predictive of cardiac tamponade, and therefore of myocardial
infarction in the future. This test is also
useful to assess the risk of bleeding
in patients with gastrointestinal hemorrhage (e.g. bleeding after colonoscopy or
gastroscopy). A low Hct may be a protective factor against
cardiac complications of sepsis. Patients with septic shock may have an impaired
Hct secondary to increased vascular permeability secondary to
inflammation-induced vasodilation, but a low Hct also indicates
reduced splanchnic blood flow in patients with septic shock.[@b14]

tumor cells are the most common target organs for bacterial

An overview can sometimes sound silly from some critics if it hasn't ever been played that often, or the person performing it is just not that good. What's the harm in saying something that probably is too silly to matter? It takes nothing away from the person performing the review to bring things around to the performance the way it should be, and it doesn't diminish the performance as a whole.

On the other hand, there's a very different point to be made with that idea from the other end of the scale. If someone does a great job with a performance but the critics are justifiably disappointed in the performance, if they see it as a failure, then there's actually a very clear reason why the critic feels that way, and that they should not be complaining. This is a matter of a critic giving their own performance one of the many possible, and not only of their own fault. If you were writing about some performance that you did yourself, you'd probably not go so far as to say "I am a great bass player," "I am not the greatest bass player in the world," or "I don't have the best rhythm guitar in the world," but you'd probably make a clear statement about what you did with the instrument or

A case report that confirms "misdeed": case and follow‑Up (2), the use of oral contraceptives.
The paper discusses the case of a woman who developed a right ovary mass diagnosed by postmortem examination. The mass was confirmed after oophorectomy and endometrial biopsy in the postoperative period. The patient was followed up, and after 6 months of oral contraceptives containing 20 micrograms of ethinyl estradiol, an ovarian mass of 25 mm was observed at laparoscopy. The mass was surgically removed. The postoperative clinical course, as well as the histopathologic, immunohistochemical, and molecular features, are discussed on a case-to-case basis.

Direction discrimination as opposed directional tuning - Part 4 (Theoretatical basis). Evidence from behavioral, neuronal and pharmacological studies: a comparative analysis and proposal for a putative mechanism.
In the previous papers of this series, we introduced the theoretical framework of directional tuning in human hearing and described how the brain uses it at low sound levels for pitch extraction. We now propose a general model for direction discrimination, which we use as a working hypothesis to explain behavioral and neural data. This model allows us to analyze these data as well as current neurobehavioral research on directional acuity (DA) in terms of this framework. The main aspects of this model, and the evidence supporting it, are described here. The model makes use of "complementary" receptive fields (RFs) located in both ears, with their tuning characteristics in the time domain and in the frequency domain. In addition, the model makes use of the principle that, in a low-frequency tone of constant pitch, perceived direction depends on the overall amplitude and frequency properties of the signal alone. In that way, it is possible to predict the response characteristics of the neural system underlying direction discrimination in terms of the frequency domain properties and of the amplitude properties of the signal. Direction by which objects will have an effect; i), 3) an idea what effects can be attributed to an object, and 4) an idea of its weight. These four dimensions do not exhaust the entire range of possible dimensions of object-effect causality, and their relevance for explanation is not limited to the context of explanatory theory. But most theories fail to explain some of the effects that are relevant to explanation, which might be expected. If explanations have the character of explanations by which effects are explained, then there are relevant effects that were not explained. An explanation only accounts for X if it has a relation that can explain X. But an explanation of Y might be relevant to explanation only if it is a relation among explanatory objects that explains Y. This is a further distinction.

The question:

What does "A new feature": do the following things?

"A new feature" is the default text displayed. If you don't override it to something different, it changes the default and you will only have the default for your question when typing what you want to ask. In other words, you should always say whatever you want to to when the screen tells you that this is the text.
"A new feature allows..." is the actual text on the screen if you override it to something that will display the list of features.
"A new feature has been introduced..." is not technically the title of the question itself but it is what you would call the text you would type if you override the text on screen.
"A new feature has been introduced" and "a new feature allow us to..." are the title. "A new feature allows us to do..." is the body.

Bacteriodinamitaceae was identified as a novel endosymbiont of anamorphic dinoflagellate Gymnodinium catenatum. The genus is comprised of endosymbiotic Bacteroidetes and anamorphic dinoflagellates, with known representatives including the biflagellate Bacteroidetes bacterium Siphonobacter iocladae and the unicellular dinoflagellate Raphidiopsis acus, as well as a member of the newly identified genus, Bacillus. The latter is a Gram-positive anamorphic bacterium that has also been isolated from the endosymbiotic dinoflagellate Prorardinium tepidum and a few other dinoflagellates. This bacterium represents a new member of the Bacillales family of the Proteobacteria, which contains the pathogenic marine bacterium Vibrio cholerae. Fungali in vitro growth inhibitants from Sirolypa fagani: identification of the metabolite responsible for inhibition of growth.
An efficient system for large-scale isolation and purification of antifungal substances derived from Sirolypa fagani has been developed. The results of aqueous and alcohol extractions showed that antifungal metabolites were mainly present within the chloroform fraction with antifungal activity of the extracts. The antifungal components responsible for antifungal activity were purified by preparative column chromatography with an ethyl acetate-formic acid solvent system and identified by a variety of spectroscopic techniques. The purification process showed that antifungal agents in the chloroform fraction have an excellent prospect of biological activity. The antifungal nature of the purified antifungal metabolite was further confirmed by the production of an EDA-3 analogue, an antagonist of the D-alanine-insensitive dipeptide-specific transport system in Candida utilis, by the action of the purified compound on its transport mechanism.

What are 'nonmonotomy classes for Python-instrumentated Python sources?' By now it should be fairly well-known that it does not make sense to instrument python code without classes, even if the instrumentation mechanism can be used for reflection. This seems to be in agreement with many other implementations - however, I'm not aware of any other implementations that have this property. The reason for this is probably the fact that one of the main reasons for using classes for instrumentation is support for reflection. However, the instrumentation mechanism does not work for classes, and hence the support for reflection is not possible.

Reflection on classes



Is there any example of reflection on classes in other Python implementations?



This can be done with PyPy , and PyPy uses classes to implement classes and provides a reflection mechanism on classes. PyPy (as of version 3.5.0), however, adds to the reflection mechanism what is essentially reflection on types. PyPy also provides the possibility to use reflection on a sub-class of any class. I don't know of any others which provide this feature.

So in essence, there are two types of reflection: reflection on types and reflection on classes. I'd like you

This image shows "Hexagons from Numerica_", "Hippu.n.a.s.m" and "Hippo.n.a.s.m" models in their original form.

The Hippu.ne.s.mu or Hippo.ne.s.mu (short for Hippo) models were introduced by Hippo during the 1990s. These models were one of the most popular toys of the 1990s, and featured several different variants. The main distinguishing feature of these models is the fact that the body in the center resembles the animal in the upper-body, which in many cases are made of resin. The two main versions feature six arms, three in front and three in back, and six legs, including one at the rear and one at the front.

The HPSM.ne.s.mu version, which is the most frequently seen version, features only three arms, two in front and one in the back. The Hippo.n.a.s.m models from the 1970s and 1980s also feature three arms in front and two in back. The only difference is that the bodies are more realistic, and their head is replaced with a red sphere.

RSS-Syrian Social National Forum Announce Early General Coordinators From the UK

The final list of the general coordinators of the annual meeting in London has been announced, with an emphasis on social justice and anti-war organizing in the US. The meeting has been called The Future of Peace, but it is likely to be renamed after the UK Foreign Secretary’s trip to meet with Syrian civil society participants.

The British government’s involvement in bringing ‘The Future of Peace’ meeting together in London is the first known instance of the British government directly promoting international anti-war protest.

The initial list of UK-based general coordinators was announced a week ago. With a total of six individuals, two are directly involved in the pro-war/pro-Israel/pro-US agenda.

The other four, including the three UK participants, are UK based, but their US political affiliations make that unlikely.

Lydia Gall, the new general coordinator of the meeting announced the UK participants as:

David Burlinson from the Friends of Syria group;

John Kingston, founder of the Coalition for Peace in the Middle East;

David Tuck, leader of Stop the War

By Lemma \[p-p1\] there are at least two paths of order 2 that contain $M$. However, if $M$ is not a path then two such paths have the same vertices and have the same length, contradiction. We conclude that $M$ is a path.

If Quack refers to an older gentleman and it is not yet time for a man to give medical advice, then do we just call it a man? The short answer is: Plenty when it comes to addressing the deficit, cutting the size of government, and eliminating subsidies to industry. But the plan does not do all of these things, and would therefore have been a non-starter in normal political circumstances.

How do we know this? The most recent version of the plan, released by the House Ways and Means Committee yesterday, is a revised version of the plan from the American Enterprise Institute titled “A Plan to Cut Taxes for Everyone.” (H/T to David Dayen of National Review.)

So let’s take a closer look.

First, the focus of the plan is to lower taxes on the middle class. What is wrong with that?

The problem with any plan to cut taxes for the middle class isn’t its policy content—there’s plenty of that in here—it is the fact that it cuts taxes so much less than Trump

This study had many design aspects with two of great implications--it opened eyes a whole host to new possibilities and new research directions. A very exciting observation was that the effect of dietary protein had only one major determinant--the protein content of the diet. The first and great hypothesis tested was that the effect of a particular protein would be independent of any other nutrients or macronutrients of the diet. The second one tested was that the effect of a protein would be mediated by the absorption/metabolism of the protein in the small and large intestine. This was a very ambitious and difficult idea to test because the physiology of absorption and metabolism was poorly understood at the time and had not been evaluated in animals. The study had three major goals. One was to determine the effect of various proteins on growth, fat deposition, and carcass quality. As a result all proteins tested enhanced growth to a varying extents. Growth was more with low-than high-protein diets, indicating that protein content does have a significant impact on growth of broiler chickens. Low dietary protein was correlated but not strongly associated with increased fat deposition, demonstrating that the effect of protein on fat deposition is indirect. [Prokinecia intestinaltis: Report and classification based only clinical considerations]. An investigation including a review of the literature.
Prokinecia intestinaltis, more commonly known as bowel-disease, is described to be a rare condition of non-infectious origin. A study was therefore undertaken of 9 patients. Of the 10 patients treated during the period 1981-1990, 6 had a colonic manifestation from 2 cases of Crohn disease, 3 of diverticulitis, and 1 each from Crohn disease of the rectum and from Crohn disease of the small intestine. The disease was clinically and therapeutically treated with broad spectrum antibiotics (cefuroxime or metronidazole) plus local resection of the affected part of the bowel. The authors also examined the clinical records of 9 cases previously published in the literature, which gave a summary of 9 cases of this disease. Of the 10 cases described, 4 were from a known patient with colorectal surgery and 6 patients were not. In three of the latter, clinical data were not available. In 5 of the 10 patients (50 per cent) the diagnosis was delayed; diagnosis was established only after the disease manifested itself clinically and the intestinal tract became dilatated

In vitro bioaccess, solutes (Vc values), permeate in brain capillaries of mice.
In order to confirm the permeability of brain capillaries, we estimated, by using single-pass radiolabeled sucrose, the solute (Vc) values across the brain capillary wall and calculated an apparent diffusion coefficient of sucrose in brain capillary. Our results showed that the apparent diffusion coefficients of sucrose were 0.7 x 10(-4) cm 2 s(-1) for brain capillaries. The brain capillary wall could pass a greater amount of sucrose than the cell membrane. This observation supports the possibility that a large number of brain capillary perforate the brain cell membranes and have a more permeable environment. Moreover, the brain capillaries, which had no blood-brain barrier, are the principal vascular system for clearance of sucrose from the brain following intraperitoneal administration.

Hippias Lattus (Greek: Ἅπιδιος ἡγνησίας ὄψεως πάρεως, ca 34 or 35–55 BC or 54–53?), better known as Lattcius, was a famous Greek comic poet, orator, writer and philosopher. Together with Callimachus (3rd century BC) and Menippus (?–? BC) he is regarded as the main founder of Western poetry of comic genre. One of his most famous satires is the famous Lattus to his son, the philosopher Plautus, dating from probably the middle of the sixth century BC.[1]

Hippias was born in Athens in ca 34 or 35 BC. He was probably taught by his father, the comic poet Aristophanes (ca 429–347 BC), who had studied comedy at the Academy of oratory (see below). His father died when Hippias was only 15 years old. Around this time he also joined the comedy theatre of the Dionys

What exactly happens after you log into Windows Live SkyDrive?

Windows Live SkyDrive is the online space that you use to store and share all of your favorite files with people over the Web. When you create a SkyDrive account for your home or work computer, you're giving it access to files on a remote computer. You can use these files, as you might use files in a local hard drive if you had that computer on a network for backup. Or you can use them to collaborate with other people and to share them with the people that you choose. Here's how it works:

On your computer, you log in with your username, password, and the email address that is associated with your SkyDrive account.

All of the files you have created since the last time you set up the account are automatically synched to other people or computers that use the same Internet connection. They will all be visible to all of the people who are connected to your network, no matter which computer they're using.

As promised at GamesplosureCon and then as further information via Erizon Studios today (read a bit here) we have an additional character in our game to look forward to in the near future.

This character is a member of The Lost Legion. The Lost Legion (otherwise known as the Lost Legion Brotherhood) was a group of adventurers who were searching for ancient treasures and artifacts on Earth 2 and other planets. This group was composed of adventurers from the United Planets (the United Earth Empire) and members from the Inner World. Many of these adventurers were wiped out in an attempt to destroy the artifacts and secret knowledge of the inner world. While much of their knowledge is now lost to time, they are still feared all over the Inner World for their ancient knowledge of alien races and their ancient technology. Many people believe they’ll make a comeback someday, which makes them an interesting addition to our game, not to mention the lore behind them.

We’re still getting the details from our story team, so we’ll be able to release more information soon. If you’re interested in learning how to play The Lost Legion Brotherhood, please join this Discord server at https://discord.gg/J9hV
