The goal was, first, to provide a simple and easy to use interface to use the most popular image decoder, PNG. This was accomplished in the Python standard library, because PIL doesn't provide an easy way for the user to specify the color space of image data before decoding (with the exception of the 'channels=1' parameter). The Python standard library offers a very good tool to read and write data in all kinds of formats - PIL does, too. The main problem with PIL and other image libraries like Pillow used for the project, is that they're written in C++ and need considerable development time to provide an interface that will allow the usage of other languages that provide simple and efficient image manipulation software (such as the Python language). AUSTIN, Calif.? The National Park Service this afternoon issued its list for 2013 fundraising awards, announcing nine finalists across the country this month as the nonprofit organization that oversees many of the nation's most visited parks makes its top 10 priorities list public.

The list will be voted on by the NPS Advisory Council, which includes influential individuals such as former New York Mayor Ed Koch, Los Angeles Mayor Antonio Villaraigosa and actress Susan Sarandon. The National Parks Conservation Association said this was a sign that the agency and its affiliates are committed to making a difference and making a case for increased conservation efforts throughout America's parks.

Q. How long could something lie underground before it turns the bottom pink or reddish-orange, a sign of methane gas?

Answer: Well, that depends on the soil.

The soil around a gas drilling is different from the soil where natural gas is trapped in the rock formations or the soil around a conventional oil exploration site.

Soil samples from the area where fracking began were collected and tested for methane gas about 15 years ago.

A state of the art laboratory at University of North Texas measured the pH of these samples and tested them for methane gas and nitrous oxide gas within three generations after drilling started in 2009.

Results of those tests have never been available because that laboratory closed down in 2009 after a new laboratory was built and the samples were transferred to its laboratory for additional tests.

The results of those tests could have been available 20 years earlier but the lab closed.

Q. Does the drilling itself produce gas, or does it produce the gas which gets collected?

Answer: The well creates the gas which is then collected.

The gas itself comes out as oil or gas.

The aim of this study was to evaluate the usefulness of prostate-specific antigen (PSA) in differentiating between transitional cell carcinomas of the bladder and normal prostatic epithelium in human prostate glands. The binding of PSA with the tissue sections and prostatic tissue was investigated in ten patients (average age 64.1 years, range 52-78 years). The binding of PSA with benign prostate tissue was less expressed than with the invasive carcinomata of prostate. With the same specimen the binding of PSA showed a higher binding capacity with transitional cell carcinomas in the group of non-obese patients and in the group of carcinomata of lower diameter. This suggests that in the non-obese patients and in cases with small tumours (less than 7cms long) the possibility of detecting these tumours with the PSA determination in the prostatic secretion is more useful. The high serum PSA level in patients with carcinoid tumours and high density PSA pattern, suggests that these tumours may be associated with PSA secretion. generally would preclude recovery on a bond. And a party to the suit on

The pericytal line (PCL) is a common lesion in animals. The PCL consists of an accumulation or collection of mucinous material in the submucosa of the distal small bowel causing a mucus plug in small lumen vessels, leading to chronic luminal ischemia of the intestinal mucosa. The mucus plug of PCL leads to fibrosis or fibroplasias. Fibroplasias in PCL have been reported as submucosal fibroblastic proliferations that may result in stricturing. Some have even described neoplasms. In the past a similar lesion could not be described in rabbits. An intestinal submucosal neoplasm was described in humans by Wernicke and Weissberger and named after these names authors. Intestinal fibroplasias may lead to a fibrothelioma in humans. Nurses and Patients Experience. Interviewees with Nursing in the USA--Interview Guide II [English].
An extensive qualitative interview study which examined and compared the experiences of nursing staffs working in four USA nursing homes. The research used a longitudinal sampling approach which involved purposive selection of participants to participate in a series of interviews, one each for a 1-month period, with a total sample of twenty-one nurses, all with at least 3 years' clinical experience. Interviews were performed using verbatim recorded telephone interviews between April and May 1996 with each nurse interviewed on their working environment with regard to how they experience their working relationships. Analysis of the interview data was guided by a process of data segmentation which involved identifying specific categories or major themes that emerged from the responses of the interviewees. The analysis focused on the category of working relationships, which includes three aspects that determine the nursing staff's experiences with their working relationship with the patient: The nurse-patient relationship, socialization in the nurse-patient relationship, and nurse-nursing practice relationship. Nuclear envelope rupture associated histoculture propagation to create functional chimeric hepatocyte spheroids.
Hepatic diseases, especially liver fibrosis, are caused by various genetic mutations that trigger hepatocyte death. Chimeric hepatic cell transplantation and cell line regeneration are strategies to treat this kind of diseases. To provide chimeric hepatocytes for cell transplantation, we developed a method for the production of functional spheroids. An in vivo method was used to produce and transfer these cells in a mouse. The spheroid-forming cells were isolated from primary hepatocytes by flow cytomtery using anti-hepatocyte-specific antigen monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) as the marker. To prepare an epithelial spheroid, hepatocytes transfected with green fluorescence protein gene were isolated using flow cytomtery with anti-human epithelial cell antigen mAb. The hepatic cells were maintained in monolayer culture on a culture dish, to which a growth medium was supplied, by using the spheroid-forming cells. Toxic stress alters liver structure even within weeks: inosyl L-alanylangate treatment.
The effect of exposure to a sub-toxic concentration of inosyl L-alanylangate (IALA) in rats is used for modelling the acute toxic stress phase of many chemical exposures. Animals were exposed to either 50 or 500 mg/kg ILA for four weeks and studied up to 12 weeks. Control rats were treated with corn oil only. Liver lesions were assessed. Histologically, exposure to 500 mg/kg ILA resulted in hypertrophy and dilated sinusoids. In the 50 mg/kg ILA group, sinusoids were also enlarged, liver cell hypertrophy, and hydropic degeneration of the hepatocytes were seen. Electron microscopy revealed hepatocyte swelling and membrane disruption in both doses. In the control group, liver cell swelling and disruption of mitochondria were seen. By 12 weeks, there were signs of liver regeneration in both groups, namely, increased regeneration as evidenced by glycogen and nuclei staining, increased mitotic figures, and increased bile duct cells. The liver histology was less severe in the ILA treated groups than with corn oil treated controls.

In situ spectrophodyas to identify phenoxy polycyclosultens and microstructures on cellulose acetate.
Phenox polycyclosultens (PPX) is an important component, which can affect the structure of cellulose acetate (CA) fibers and cause fiber defects, which affect the mechanical strength and service life of textile. However, it is not easy to identify PPX from fiber defect by visual inspection. In this research, two-dimensional and four-dimensional surface spectral imaging was used to identify PPX on CA fiber surface in situ. The surface images showed bright point on the fibers with a diameter in range of 3-5 μm. After treatment with NaOH solution and EDTA, the fibers were changed from light red to dark, the black spots were identified as PPX. The average of dark spots number on fibers and the number of black spots in 100 fibers were 20.56±4.36 and 33.04±12.33. These results demonstrated the feasibility of using surface spectral imaging to identify PPX on CA fibers.

Abstract

Endorendolar resection was first described in 1951 by G.A. Dorn and T.S. Leung. It is an aggressive surgery for treating advanced cancers of the oral tongue and oropharynx. However, the role of the resectional path, that of the surgeon, and the outcome of these radical operations have never been evaluated. In our retrospective study, over a 10-year period from 1995 to 2004, we analyzed the resectional path of 45 patients (mean age 68.5+/-10.6) who underwent endorendolar resection for locally advanced cancers. The path was evaluated according to the current standard. The follow-up varied from 2 months to 30 months (mean 7.6 months). The rate of tumor control was 63.3%, and the 5-year survival was 55.6%. In the analysis of recurrence, only 11.1% of patients had local recurrence, and the disease-free survival was 100%. Dance Floor Art - a new genre born for DJs (but the best in a great number of music categories)

A DJ never looks back

When a DJ leaves the dance floor, it seems that he goes out as he was then ... not to a club, or a restaurant. He might drink a little, but not to be tipsy or drunk, he might order a coffee from the machine rather than eat breakfast at home, but not a morning after. Instead he just goes out in his normal dress code for the day, without all the extras. There is perhaps a little more than you might expect to be done with your clothes on, but nothing too fussy. He gets ready at home by brushing his hair, folding his underpants, buttoning his shirt and doing that whole routine over again. But he does not want to do this a second time. So he does what I do - he uses a little scissors to cut out the dance floor pieces of his clothing. Then, and this is my great pleasure, he throws them away. I throw them away to keep myself from re-entering in them, but the moment he takes them out of the drawer, off my shoulders, I want to throw them away for no reason.

An efficient nonparameteted Cox step algorithm procedure designed that has maximum theoretical convergence rate is proposed for identifying the minimum error estimates for the survival models with continuous and discrete time response. This is a key step towards the analysis of data with non-monotone response which are of increasing importance. Applications of models such as the Cox model with the hazard ratio as the link function and the binary response model with a logistic link are of increasing interest in medical science as a means to identify prognostic variables or predict events that are likely to be observed in medical research. Due to the complex dependence structure, the identification of the optimal parameter of the survival models that minimizes the error is a challenging problem. The objective of this paper is twofold. The first is twofold: to propose and to test a method for constructing a sequence that converges to the optimal solution of minimizing the asymptotic error, when the sample size tends to infinity. The second is to propose a statistical test procedure to evaluate the adequacy of the resulting fitted regression model. The proposed method and its statistical tests are evaluated with a simulation study as well as with a real data example.

A new, fully human genome has paved many new biological exploraton techniques because researchers hope more comprehensive data eventually will help solve other diseases, including those facing Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, and other neurodegenerative ailments. The data, published Monday in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS), show the genes of at-risk human populations that are linked with dementia risk.

Genetic and other information on tens of thousands of individuals who have had dementia was compared with the genomes of the same people without the disorder, a rare and rapidly expanding disease that usually occurs after the age of 65 and is the leading cause of dementia in older people. The research, conducted by a team of researchers led by David Reich of the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard, shows a clear link between the number of genes with a change associated with Alzheimer’s disease and the amount of risk, or potential for a dementia diagnosis. Shelter & Rescue Animals (SS)
The San Rafael City Public Assistance League(SPA) is the first and only nonprofit organization assisting local and non-local San Rafael citizens in times of need to provide emergency and short-term animal help. The San Rafael Society of Humane Societies (SRSHS) has been providing animal care under the Animal Control Act to a variety of public and private organizations, most significantly the San Rafael City Public Assistance League, since its founding in 1974.

Since 1996, SSPA has provided non-emergency animal help (such as food, water, medicine, and vaccinations). Because such help is provided when the organization is overwhelmed by emergency animal help requests, an animal help fund is maintained to insure that all emergency animals receive medical care or veterinary care.

The goal of SS is for animals who are experiencing suffering or need help to be given the care they need, in a humane manner that is in line
with their welfare needs and provides positive reinforcement for their behavior.

If you would like to join the SS animal help program, please contact the San Rafael City Public Assistance League (SRPS-564) at (408) 561-3570.

Sensitivity, or sensity
An individual (whether natural or mutant and alive during growth/sexual propagation) whose genetic material, at its most basic level, resembles the DNA of a particular type. Some mutants exist with the genes of the mutant being copied from parents bearing normal genes, the rest being “revertants” whose genes have mutated and thus are “unaltered”. Therefore, each gene has two versions; one from parents and another from the mutant. These two versions may differ because of mutations, deletions, and insertions. Thus, each gene may code for many different proteins and their respective functions, as well as for the genetic information used to produce the protein or the “stamp” that specifies its unique amino acid sequence; the specific information that makes each protein a specific function is called the protein “signature”. (See also gene and molecular biology.)
Each gene contains only the specific genetic information, but not the regulatory information which controls expression of the genetic information during growth, development and sexual reproduction. Mutations in one or more genes make an organism “defective” and may result in a number of deleterious effects when the gene is mutated in a significant proportion of an organism

Craniomodulators based solely on cranelipomine I3 receptor binding assay.
In this study, we examined the effects of 2 receptor blockers which might modulate the release of growth, differentiation and cell survival/proliferation related substances. The cranelipomine I3 receptor antagonist 1,4 methyl-6-phenyl-1,2,5,6 tetrahydropyridine-3-carbonitrile (3-CPATN) and the I1 receptor antagonist flupilocaine (FLU) were used. The I1 and I3 receptor antagonists were found to modulate their own concentrations in the range of 10(-6)M and 10(-5)M, respectively. The inhibition of growth factor-induced cell and tissue proliferation activity in a concentration-dependent manner and the decrease in a dose-dependent manner of the expression of cell death related substances such as caspase 3 and caspase 8 were observed. The obtained results make it possible to assume that the I1 and I3 receptors might be the important targets for the treatment of certain cell proliferations diseases such as cancer, atherosclerosis, chronic rheumatic and cardiovascular diseases.

[Acupanacial procedure based rehabilitation (ABRY-WISC--based) based on the model of the WISC test battery--application].
The article contains data concerning two types of rehabilitation (a) a method of treatment of a children's cerebral palsy in accordance with the model of the WISC test battery and (b) the procedure of acupanacial procedure based on the model of the WISC test battery. The model of the WISC test battery developed originally by the authors of the article was used to define and illustrate the ABRY-WISC--based method of treatment of a cerebral palsy in the age group from 3 to 7. The ABRY--WISC--based method involves a multiplanar reconstruction of the brain, a complex treatment using brain-tonometry, complex and precise treatment of a cerebral palsy using the WISC test battery, as well as complex, individualized training of patients' ability to move, with a high level of accuracy--the method of ABRY-WISC--based rehabilitation is used. It is expected that the results of this study will be a reference for practical treatment of cerebral palsy based on the model of the WISC test battery.

Cigales and mezzogges
(c) by John Burch:
One night I had a wonderful evening with my friend Steve,
who was, at the time, a practicing Buddhist. We were discussing various philosophical topics for quite a while, and eventually came up the matter of mezzogges, the threefold division of the Buddhist world-view, namely, svarajdhutaso (monks), puchendran (lay brothers), and gurusvacaso (luminaries).
When Steve told me his opinion of the threefold division, I decided that it was time to share my own.
I have had the pleasure of serving as the Chief Eunuch at two different sanghas, and I have been able to observe the workings of a number of the threefold divisions. The first two divisions are fairly easy to understand in a layperson's mind.
The third division seems to require a great deal more study, because the subject of mezzogges is, in some places, still a taboo. In others, it is considered so difficult to discuss that people will not go to such great lengths in preparation.
In my opinion, mezzogges are a very interesting subject

An unusual pattern in ovarian tumor spectrum - diagnostic pitfalls from literature surveys in Tunisia : 3 cases.
Ovarian clear cell carcinomas are aggressive tumors with an overall high recurrence rate. However, they often mimic low-grade serous ovarian carcinoma. A complete re-examination of all diagnostic materials from the different institutions in Tunisia was performed for the evaluation of the diagnosis of clear cell ovarian tumor, in order to improve the therapeutic strategy. Among the 3 patients identified in the survey, the diagnoses of clear cell adenocarcinoma and clear cell carcinoma were made. Both had a previous diagnosis of low-grade serous ovarian carcinoma. The diagnosis of clear cell ovarian carcinoma is also included based on the immunohistochemical examination of cytokeratins in the paraffin embedded material. Our findings demonstrated that there are two major pathologies within the ovarian clear cell tumor spectrum: clear cell carcinoma (CCC) and clear cell adenocarcinoma (CCA). These 2 entities can be easily distinguished from each other by their different immunohistochemistry features, and also by their characteristic morphological and molecular characteristics.

Determination techniques and quality of information of aflatoxictomogenic *S aldrovanishii* in urine by an enzymatic method.
The aim of the present study was to establish a simple method to measure aflatoxins in urine samples. The most common sample matrices tested were urine and faeces from humans, cattle, pigs and chickens. The method was based on an enzymatic degradation for aflatoxins and determination of the resulting fluorescent substances by spectrophotometry. In addition, liquid samples containing at least 250 microg/l of aflatoxins were fortified with aflatoxin B1, B2, G1 and G2 at two different concentrations levels (from 10 to 1000 microg/l), and analysed by an enzymatic method. The results obtained using this method, especially for urine samples from cattle, pigs and chicken, were found to be better than those obtained by a modified high performance liquid chromatography with fluorescence detection. The ending might be a surprise. The book might be left behind.

Wolves in the Door by Sarah Hall is an emotional, highly accomplished tale revolving around an orphan girl's search for a father figure, and about the complicated relationship between her mother and her best friend. One can also make the point that we have already had enough of _dénouement_ in drama as the result of the traditional _fait divers_ with its attendant irony. And finally, of course, a good deal of comedy would be a waste since we would have to write a new "problem" as part of each new act (as they do occasionally in Aristophanes and Euripedes).

Finally there is the second and more important difference: _dénouement_ and _conclusion_ are not simply one and the same thing, as the editors suggest, though in practice they can be. Dénouement requires a resolution of the tension which has begun the drama, a new beginning. This is not merely the product of what we know as the "problem" or "plot" as already defined in act I and act two, but of the development which has taken place in each of these acts during a series of exchanges in which the protagonists themselves have been trying to get at this central tension—tension which would be resolved in act three and acted out in act four as the "resolution" of

Hippurate anion receptors activate calcium responses under high salt and hyperthyrotria by binding to a distinct set of sites.
Calcium-sensing receptor (CASR) is a member of the G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) family. It is expressed in various tissues and is responsive to high intracellular Ca(2+) levels or calcium influx [1,2]. Here, we investigate the effects of calcium on CASR signaling in rat pancreatic acinar cells and in pancreatic β-cells at high salt concentrations and hyperthyroid condition. The calcium response to CASR activation is increased in the pancreatic acinar cells and pancreatic β-cells under hyperthyroid conditions. At high salt exposure conditions, there is a significant increase in CASR-mediated calcium response in the pancreatic acinar cells. However, the calcium response to CASR activation remains unaffected in the β-cells at high salt conditions. This evidence indicates that CASR is activated by binding to a distinct set of sites in pancreatic acinar cells and pancreatic β-cells under different conditions.

A typical process involves taking two distinct regions of an IC mask, called a pre-amorphous silicon (PASI) and a wafer lithographically, and then creating a pattern for the IC using the two masks in parallel operations. The wafer pattern is then transferred to the PASI to create a mask pattern that, when used with conventional lithography machinery, creates a mask for the IC. The mask pattern will define the topography for the features of the IC and will define the spacing and size of the features. The mask pattern for a given IC might be created for the purpose of creating a pattern to be transferred to the wafer and might or might not have any features other than those on the wafer.
Another typical process involves using a high resolution electron beam lithography (EBL) machine to create a mask pattern for a PASI wafer in which the feature size is measured in the micron or nanometer range. The wafer pattern produced by the EBL machine has the effect of changing the topography for the photoresist in PASI by shrinking small features within the mask pattern in relation to the size of the feature before the PASI was formed into the mask structure. Chinatown – Chinatown is a thriving shopping area downtown. The streets are home to a variety of different ethnic restaurants and businesses. There are many Chinese restaurants scattered throughout the area of the district such as a large noodle shop, many taquerias, a bakery, and food vendors.

Pit boss has long and rich memory



our man had a big old mousy thing that looked a bit like a pit bull. He was a real sweetheart. His name was Sami, and I never forgot to tip my hat to him when I was in town. That's usually where his memory gets the best of me! But if Sami could see me today, his mouth would probably be open! His memory just got a whole lot better!





So after my visit, I didn't see Sami for a while, and he didn't come out to greet me. But all is not lost because there was my friend, Mr. H. J. Wiggs, who lives two doors down. Sami's friend, H. J. (I believe he didn't actually have a last name) just happens to be my friend. He has a big brownie point between his eyes, just like Sami. He had been married to my wife's sister for the entire time that I lived in their house. My wife knows H. J. from that time in the house but doesn't remember her family, or anything that ever happened in the house before us; except for the day she and I got married.

It really looks weird seeing photos like the video uploaded last September at an abandoned military plant.

It’s not that far fetched.

Back in 2015 a group of activists got a group of engineers to show off the work of some of the most dangerous, yet most lucrative, arms makers on the planet. The video is called The Dangers Of Nuclear Power and has a lot of interesting content, including videos of the process of building these weapons.

“These guys have got the power and money to make this crap,” said Andrew Cockburn at the time, and he wasn’t wrong.

Back in September 2014, when the video was posted, North Korea wasn’t the only nation building nuclear reactors. China also had one just a few miles away on the coast. That’s the first time I’ve ever called these new technologies dangerous, if they’re building reactors we’re talking about something that can cause a massive regional war.

If I were a betting man, I’d bet that if Iran has a nuclear reactor they would detonate it at their own uranium enrichment facility and cause even worse destruction than what was caused in 2013.

In theory, for the sake of avoiding misunderstandings, one who makes a card must, when speaking to another and another, give the same message for the card as he does for the other person. In the past, as the recording density of a magnetic recording medium such as a hard disk increases, there is a problem that the recording bit size decreases correspondingly and that the reproducing bit size is decreased, causing an increase in the possibility of a so-called read head crash (i.e., head drop).
Further, in the head crash, while data is being read by the reproducing head to output the data to the outside from a magnetic recording head on the magnetic recording medium such as a hard disk, the data is often recovered in an incorrect state. In such a case, it is desirable that the recovering time be decreased.
In addition, if the recording density becomes higher than the read bit recording density on the track with a narrow interval. As a result, a so-called crosstalk occurs, causing a problem that data for a target bit position is read out erroneously, which is a significant problem.
In addition, to improve the compatibility with a recording/reproducing unit which does not use magnetic recording heads, there is a need for a technology allowing simultaneous read/write of two or more tracks on a magnetic recording medium such as a hard disk at the same time.

How can we say "he went to the cinema" without using he did?

I understand that when speaking about movies he is saying he went to the cinema. But is it correct to say "he(she) went to the cinema?" Is it also correct to say something like "she had gone to the cinema" in this kind of situations? If it is, why?
Thanks ahead.

A:

To say "he/she went to the cinema" you can use the demonstrative pronoun and the preposition.
He went to the cinema because something happened happened to him.
She has taken to going to the cinema because she saw something happen to her.

The difference is that you use "he" because you want to make an affirmation of a person you know, while you use "she" because you want to make the affirmation for an unspecified person. Of course, this is not limited to the cinema! There are many other examples. 
To make an affirmation of someone you don't know, you can use a third-person pronoun, like "it (she) was".

where x.shape is (2, 500) 
for i in range(2):
    for j in range(x.shape[1]):
        x[i, j] = 2*i+j

and so on...

Why is it necessary to use a for loop here? When x is 2*2*2, what will happen if just an index like: x[0, 0, 0] = 4? Why not x[0, 0, 0]=0?

A:

This is because
x[0, 0, 0] = 4

is interpreted as
[x[0, 0, 0], 0, 0] = 0

because of the commutator  x[(0, 0, 0)], instead of x[0, (0, 0, 0)] = 0 as you expected, for it uses index 0 as the first element as

A recent study found that more than a third of college students were using substances, the latest survey from the College Student Drug Use Study by the National Survey on Drug Use and Health shows.

The study analyzed national trends from 2006 to 2013, finding that the number of students who use drugs is declining, but that the problem is still pervasive: 21 percent of U.S. college students use drugs.

"We don’t think it’s going to be as large as before," said Jeffrey Selingo, the lead author on the study, of Michigan State University. "But we do think that it’s the same percentage of students using drugs across all demographic categories or characteristics."

A third of students who use drugs report using stimulants, such as Adderall, Ritalin and ecstasy, the survey found. It also revealed that most commonly students used opioids, including illicit drugs and prescription medicine, and that more than half said they had used heroin.

About 10 percent of students used cocaine, the report said.

But as far as the students' social and personality characteristics, only about half reported using substances at least monthly, and nearly a quarter only drank alcohol and about 15 percent smoked pot.

Alfa Romeo Cucuruzela is a superb example of the workmanship and engineering skill it took the Italian company to put together: an outstanding example of the car that is to this day so admired among us.

Alfa Romeo Cucuruzela is a superb example of the workmanship and engineering skill it took the Italian company to put together: an outstanding example of the car that is to this day so admired among us.

The car's production was in the mid to late 1960's, when it featured a 4.0L V6 (250+ horsepower) and rear-wheel drive. The top engine was a 5.9L V8 with 4.56 gears. The engine was mounted by hand in the back in the lower rear quarter panel, with the drivetrain and the suspension being driven with a push/pull mechanism.

It was this style of vehicle that would become so well known as the 'fastback' - Italian for 'fastback' and the name given in the States for the 'Baja Cab'-style 'Cucuruzela' (see the Baja Cab vs. The Cucuruzela)

C. Srinivasan

C. A. Srinivasan was an Indian politician and trade unionist. He was the founder president of the Indian Railway Workers Union.

Career
Srinivasan took over as the president of the Indian Railway Workers Union when it had a membership of just 2,000 in 1938. Srinivasan was later elected President of Indian Trade Union Congress (ITUC) in 1942 and President of Bombay Trade Union Congress (BTUC) in 1945. He was elected as President of All India Trade Union Congress (AITUC) in 1947. He was the founding president of Bombay District Congress Committee and then Bombay District Committee of the Congress Party. He was a member of Bombay Legislative Assembly from 1953 to 1957. He was elected as member of Bombay Legislative Assembly from 1953 to 1957. He took charge as the president of Bombay Pradesh Congress Committee in 1957. In this role he was also elected as President of Bombay District Congress Committee. He died in 1957 in hospital.

Surgical treatment of pulmonary metastases and pulmonary embolism following nephrectomy: the surgical experience at San Francisco General Hospital.
Surgical resections of renal cell carcinomas and pulmonary metastases are being performed more frequently because of improvements in perioperative anesthesia and surgical techniques. This study evaluates our experience with surgical resections for pulmonary metastases and associated pulmonary embolism after nephrectomy. His father, a Jewish refugee, was murdered in 1914 in Lemberg. A year later, Schmucki enrolled at the Technische Hochschule München, and attended the technical school (Schulverein) in the city of Munich. He received his degree on November 30, 1921 with high honors in the German university school system. He was married to the former Gretl Weigel; they had no children.

Schmucki worked at a number of various universities and industrial institutes in West Germany, first at the Tum and later  at the Institute for Chemical and Biological engineering of the Technische Universität München (Tum). Biotreatment of COD wastewater using an efficient biosorbent.
This research aims to improve nitrogen removal efficacies of sludge by applying the biosorbent, a mixed fungal-bacteria consortium (P. verrucosum x P. chrysosporium F1-2), to chemical oxygen demand (COD) wastewater treatment. The mixed consortium was obtained by growing and acclimatizing P. verrucosum (F2) in an aerated mineral medium. The mixed consortium showed a strong ability in COD degradation under aerobic conditions. The degradation rates for COD in the mixed consortium were increased from 33 to 83.7% without increasing the organic loading rate (OLR) and without reducing the COD content in the treated wastewater. In order to enhance the COD removal of the mixed consortium, biocation and biotreatment treatments were performed. Biotreatment increased the removal rate more effectively than the conventional biotreatment method. Biotreatment showed a higher removal rate than the conventional method, which attributed to the higher adsorption of the mixed consortium on Fe/Al-based magnetic granular bed. Fluorescence spectroscopy for the study of protein folding.
Recent progress in fluorescence spectroscopy has made it possible to measure many properties of an ensemble average of a molecular species on a fast timescale. This review provides a detailed description of the properties for one such property, fluorescence anisotropy, that are routinely measured with several spectroscopic methods. In addition, the review describes the types of experiments used for the study of folding, an approach that has been used to elucidate key aspects of the folding of a small number of proteins. The fluorescence spectroscopy community is rapidly moving into the realm of a wide-field spectroscopy, and it is important that the principles and interpretation of these measurements are properly understood before they are incorporated into the study of protein folding.

Pulmonary tuberculosis in an adult with a previous diagnosis of non-malignant lung disease: a clinical and radiological study.
This is the first experience concerning a non-malignant lung disease in tuberculosis. A 56-year-old man was admitted to the hospital with progressive haemoptysis. Chest x-ray and high resolution computed tomography (HRCT) detected active bilateral lung disease and pleural thickening, which was suggestive for mesothelioma or lung cancer. Bronchoscopy with biopsy confirmed pulmonary tuberculosis by tissue histology. Treatment with rifampicin was initiated and the patient underwent successful pleural biopsy and palliative thoracoscopic sympathectomy to relieve the thoracic haemoptysis.

Tailoring the magnetic anisotropy of FeNb alloys via an electrochemically controlled synthesis technique.
The preparation of FeNb alloys with tunable magnetic anisotropy is essential for the design of permanent magnet devices such as motor and storage media. We have successfully synthesized this family of FeNb alloys by electrochemically synthesizing single crystals from the high-T c phase as the parent alloy. We have further tailored the spin polarization through the choice of the electrolytic potential. The alloys have a low coercive field (<50 Oe) among the non-stoichiometric FeNb alloys reported to date. The lattice parameter, the volume fraction and the magnetic structure of the FeNb alloys were characterized by neutron powder diffraction, microstructural analysis and magnetization measurements. The magnetic anisotropy was shown to depend substantially on the electrochemical synthesis conditions but is only weakly dependent on the parent crystal lattice. The tunability of the spin polarization allows for the design of devices that can perform fast switching and high energy densities. Q:

What does "this time" mean?

Could you please kindly tell me what does "this time" mean in this context?

I had to stay at home on Tuesday morning and then I got my job.   
I have to stay at home on Tuesday morning and then I have a job.     
I had to stay at home on Tuesday morning and then this time I had a job.     

A:

You can be using this time metaphorically by saying to someone "I have to stay home on Tuesday morning and this time I had to stay home."  This way, you are indicating that you did not have to stay home because it was already a thing that has happened, so it is a thing that is happening right now and you do not have to plan for it.  As with a real time thing, you usually are in control of something because it is happening now.
Here, the word that has to stay is "housewife" and the other word is "job."  By the way, the "this time" could be a relative term that describes the time now that the job is happening in relationship to the day before.

[Epidemiology of human schistosomiasis].
Schistosoma haematobium and S. mansoni are the causative agents of urinary and genital bladder and genital tract schistosomiasis. In Africa, these diseases are responsible for up to 300,000 deaths annually and the morbidity reaches 15 million people in the endemic area. There are five species of cercaria: S. haematobium, S. mansoni, S. japonicum, S. mekongi and S. mekongi-nakazakii. Infection of humans is due to water contact and a water-dependent, cercarial life cycle. The snail species are the intermediate host, and the trematode can penetrate human skin through intact or injured skin, or through mucosa lesions. Human exposure to Schistosoma is more common among sub-Saharan Africa countries than the others, including Europe and America. In Brazil, most cases occur in the southeast. The infection is more common in children, females and the indigenous people. The disease is endemic mainly in areas of tropical forest or savannah. However, there are cases reported from the north and the west of the country especially in urban areas.

Determination of the relative effects of different forms of dietary fat on the absorption and utilization of dietary cholesterol in humans.
The effects of varying fat content in the diet on the absorption and metabolism of cholesterol and the plasma lipids in humans in vivo were studied. Two forms of dietary fat were used, a high cholesterol diet consisting of 10% (w/w) coconut fat and a low cholesterol diet consisting of 10% (w/w) butter fat (with an addition of 3% cottonseed oil) both in conjunction with coconut oil supplementation. A high cholesterol diet produced significant increases in the plasma cholesterol levels by about 25% in comparison to rats fed a low cholesterol diet. This increase in cholesterol by the high cholesterol diet was accompanied by significantly larger increases in the plasma triacylglycerols than in the total plasma levels of lipids. Similar differences between coconut fat and butter fat diets were also observed. Compared to low dietary cholesterol, the increases in plasma lipids were about 30% in rats fed coconut fat and about 30% in rats fed butter fat diets. A difference between coconut fat and butter fat was seen in the extent of incorporation of the two dietary fat fractions into the cholesterol, which was significant in cholesterol metabolism after 24 h in rats fed the high cholesterol

A review of the use of oral diazepines in the management of anxiety and agitation in older patients.
Oral diazepam has been administered by a variety of means to treat the common problems of medical in-home care of people in their old age. There is much evidence supporting the use of oral diazepam in the management of the symptoms of anxiety and agitation. A short-term trial demonstrated that diazepam given under supervision significantly reduced the levels of anxiety, agitated behaviour, and agitation in institutionalized elderly patients. This was shown to be more effective in this population than the benzodiazepines clobazam or lorazepam. Diazepam may be helpful in reducing the anxiety and agitation of many older patients, especially those with dementia when they are admitted to an in-patient care institution. Care home staff should be aware that some patients with dementia, particularly those for whom a history of cognitive impairment is a predictor of a likely response to treatment, may not demonstrate significant improvement in behavioural symptoms with diazepam or clobazam.

Nursing-led rehabilitation programs--a systematic review.
Rehabilitation programs provided by different nursing services are increasingly being offered in hospitals, community hospitals, and private nursing homes. The purpose of the present article is to critically review nursing-led rehabilitation programs (NRP) and present their main characteristics. The literature regarding the implementation of NRP in in-hospital care is summarized. Inpatient NRP may be used with varying degrees of consistency. In the majority of case studies, this type of service was associated with positive outcomes with respect to patients, the nursing profession, and other professions involved in patient care. NRP are an option for improving in-hospital patient care. The results of this review should be considered while planning and implementing NRP.

Where MyMaxWidth is just a constant from one of the property sheets

BALB/c mice were immunized subcutaneously with 10 micrograms of dinitrophenol (DNP)-keyhole limpet hemocyanin conjugated to keyhole limpet lectin. These cells underwent cytotoxic T-cells--specific cytotoxicity against tumor cells, but not against sheep red blood cells or irrelevant antigens. The cells which produced killer activity were used to immunize BALB/c mice and to obtain lymphocyte enriched population that formed an immunoreactive cytotoxic T-cell population. Polaritons with zero effective mass in a one-dimensional periodic medium: an effective Hamiltonian formalism.
We present a general scheme of modeling and treating the collective modes of a one-dimensional periodic medium using a Hamiltonian formalism based on a modified version of the usual Bloch equations. Our approach is to derive as many as possible zero-energy excitations of the collective modes, that is, excitations which are exactly delocalized; the Hamiltonian of the system is then made up of all low-energy modes of the model. We apply this formalism to a particular case of collective mode in an optically-active two-dimensional photonic bandgap structure, and provide a numerical illustration for two relevant values of the parameter of the medium: either of the dielectric coefficient of the periodic medium or an effective dielectric constant.

Shelley Adelson will soon be a homeowner again. The 35-year-old New Yorker will move in with her boyfriend in Brooklyn’s Clinton Hill neighborhood as part of a settlement agreement stemming from her two-year legal battle against the bank she’s accused of defrauding. Her ex-husband, actor and musician Nick Neufer, sued for divorce in 2008 and argued that Adelson owed back salary from his time on “House Hunters.”

The suit came down late last year following a series of financial moves. Adelson is said to have paid more than $150,000 worth of house payments on behalf of her boyfriend before paying off the house—an amount that should cover her debt, according to court papers. It also led to a cash-out provision: Adelson has agreed to pay $8 million in cash to the bank when she vacates the house.

According to New York Daily News, the settlement was “amicable but has been on hold since Adelson accused mortgage lender M&T Bank of mortgage fraud last October”—a month after Neufer sued the bank for $2.2 billion. A settlement has yet to be reached.

[Study of the mechanism associated to the regulation of bone mineral density in mice].
A simple method was proposed to study the mechanism of mineralization in mouse. After treatment with vitamin D3, calcium was absorbed by the intestinal, bone metabolism was altered. Then the calcium was deposited in the bone, bone mineral density was increased. Bone mineral density was decreased after treatment with vitamin D3 and calcium in mice. The change of bone mineral density in mice after treatment with vitamin D3 was different in the different periods of time from 4 mo to 12 mo of age. Bone mineral density was increased after treatment of vitamin D3 between 4 and 6 mo age, but after treatment of vitamin D3, bone mineral density decreased between 6 and 8 mo age. The change of bone mineral density in mice after treatment with vitamin D3 in the different periods of time may depend on the difference of calcium absorption by the intestinal. The mechanism on the effect of vitamin D3 on bone formation depends on the change of calcium deposition in bone and bone mineral density.

A novel method for studying the effects of the dopamine transporter inhibitor GBR 12909 in normal as well as in dopamine receptor supersensitive animals is described. The present methodology combines an in vivo model, for testing the effects of acute administration, and an in vitro model, for testing chronic treatment, of one dopaminergic compound to the dopaminergic system. It relies on the use of an inhibitor of the dopamine transporter, GBR 12909, alone or in combination with drugs that would be expected to potentiate GBR 12909 effects and therefore interfere in its mechanism of action, in order to assess the involvement of the dopamine transporter in some aspect of Parkinsonian symptoms occurring after chronic exposure with different dopaminergic agents. Using an in vitro model, we found that the interaction of GBR 12909 with the dopamine transporter depends upon a particular dose and the duration of treatment of each dopaminergic drug, since the effects of GBR 12909 are different on brain dopamine uptake in normal rats as compared with dopamine receptor supersensitive animals. Our findings suggest that the mechanism of GBR 12909, especially when used in combination with some of the dopamine receptor agonists used in the dopamine supersensitive animals, may not be the dopamine transporter, and therefore suggest the possibility that GBR

Ectopic adrenal rest tumour associated with a familial melanoma syndrome.
A 30-year-old woman was admitted for evaluation of a right adrenal mass. Laboratory studies revealed an excessive excretion of cortisol (0.7 mg/day) with a low plasma corticotrophin concentration (19.7 fmol/L). No adrenal tumor was found on the computed tomography scan. She had no symptoms of pheochromocytoma or any other adrenal disease. The finding of cortisol hypersecretion, in absence of any evidence of adrenal tumor and cortisol suppression test and with the lack of pheochromocytoma, led to the diagnosis of ectopic adrenal rest tumour associated with a familial melanoma syndrome-the MEN 2B syndrome. The patient was followed by an adrenal tumor screening program. After 2 years, the screening was repeated. The adrenal tumor was found to be a benign adrenal rest tumour and a familial melanoma syndrome was excluded. Ectopic adrenal rest tumour associated with a familial melanoma syndrome is rare. It should be excluded in patients who have hypercortisolism and who do not have adrenal tumors.

The Anaheim Kings (formerly known as the Los Angeles Kings) are an American National Hockey League team based in Anaheim, California. In 2002, the Kings became the first professional sports franchise to be based in southern California. The Kings hold record attendance in the history of the NHL, at an average of over 19,000 people per game.

History
The franchise was created on or around May 15, 1979, with the purpose of taking the place of the Detroit Red Wings and the California Red Wings of the World Hockey Association. In June 1979, the franchise began play as the Los Angeles Kings. The team was initially owned by three Los Angeles businessmen, and the team had no uniform or equipment of its own. The Kings played at the Civic Auditorium before playing at the Anaheim Civic Center later that month. Before the start of the 1982–83 season, the Kings moved into their own home, Anaheim Ice Arena. The team was awarded to the state of California by Governor Pete Wilson.

Ralph Lauren quietly dropped the final details on its planned $12 billion downtown Miami headquarters project, which was revealed last week in reports that said it will generate $1.8 billion in economic impact.

In its final bid presentation Wednesday, the office-store apparel giant outlined the project in greater detail.

The details were provided to South Florida Business by the developer, who declined to reveal financial information because it isn’t eligible for state records.


It’s also unclear how long construction of the building will take and the size of the budget, officials said.

The company has not said specifically what it plans to call the new location, which could bring in more than 150,000 square feet of office space and about 5 million square feet of retail.

Ralph Lauren is building a new store in suburban Washington that’s expected to bring in $750 million in sales, according to the company.

If it lands, the Miami deal represents at least one of the crown jeweled jewels of a multimillion-dollar real estate enterprise.

The company also said it would create about 40 positions during construction, more than triple the current workforce of around 2,000.

Q:

What's the relationship between a.out, b.out, and other C library files in linux?

What's the relationship between a.out, b.out, and other C library files in linux and how can I understand the differences between those three files?

A:

a.out and b.out are static archives.  They are built off of the same source code, but the name may be different.  For example, a static library can be named libfoo.a, while a source file that has been compiled into an executable with the -o flag and uses LD_RUN_PATH=. will use the -o .lib extension.
.o - binary object file (a simple compiled to machine code, similar to a C program, e.g. your program)  
.lib - a static library, usually produced with gcc, but produced by many programs.  
A.out, and other C libraries in Linux are written in ANSI C.  
B.out, are C++, which is the result from compiling with C++ compiler.  e.g. g++.
In Linux, the difference between the name, the extension

Personality constructs were assessed in a sample of 141 undergraduates (66 males, 75 females, M age=20.7) using the Temperament and Character Inventory (TCI; Costa & McCrae, 1989a) and the NEO Five Factor Indicator Inventory (NEO-FFI; Costa & McCrae, 1986; Costa et al., 1992) which has a similar structure.

1. Field of the Invention
The invention relates to a method for operating a memory element comprising at least a storage capacitor and a discharge transistor having a control electrode and a storage electrode, wherein the discharge transistor includes a control capacitor.
2. Description of the Prior Art
In order to optimize the utilization of memory cells, the control-voltage supply of each storage capacitor of a memory cell is changed during the operation of the storage capacitor. In so doing, the control capacitance of the storage capacitor is used more effectively than the storage capacitance of each storage capacitor of a memory cell, which, however, has a comparatively low operating efficiency. Since this also decreases the data storage capability, the efficiency of the control capacitance of an individual storage capacitor and the storage capacitance of a memory cell are inversely related. For this reason, a memory cell with a high operating efficiency, i.e., a memory cell having a low control capacitance, is necessary. The higher the operating efficiency is, the lower the storage capacitance is and vice versa.

Amino acids at the N-terminus of the human N-methyl-D-aspartate receptor: molecular and pharmacological characterization.
To clarify the physiological roles of the N-terminal amino acids of the human N-methyl-D-aspartate (NMDA) receptor, we determined the molecular properties of NMDA-sensitive glycine sites on the NMDA receptor glycine-binding site with the use of site-directed mutagenesis of amino acid residues flanking the highly conserved glycine in the receptor. The mutated glycine sites were expressed in Xenopus oocytes. Each of three sites (E14, D17(1), and D17(2)) yielded a glycine receptor with the pharmacological properties of the wild-type receptor. The single point mutations showed that both serine and arginine residues in the NTA sequence, but not lysine or histidine residues, were essential for binding [3H], but not for inhibition of [3H]glycine release, indicating that glycine binding was not influenced by proline, hydroxyproline, methionine, or cysteine in the NTA sequence.

To investigate the expression of p53 and c-Myc in epithelial and stromal tumor tissues in parotid gland. The parotid gland specimens from 42 cases of benign parotid tumors and 42 cases of malignant parotid tumors were used. The frozen tissue specimens were divided into stromal and epithelial tumor tissues. The expressions of p53 and c-Myc by the immunohistochemical assay of parotid tissues were studied. The results were positive in 19 of 42 (44.2%) benign parotid tumors and positive in 29 of 42 (68.4%) malignant parotid tumors. There are several instances in the chemical-processing industry where it is desirable to remove particles entrained in an air stream at relatively high temperatures, for example, in the range of 2000.degree. Protein kinase C

Protein kinase C is an intracellular signaling molecule identified in 1978 (Bayer et al., 1978). This protein was found to belong to the non-genomic kinase subfamily of protein kinases, and to be activated by phosphorylation. These kinases are involved in various signal transduction pathways that have been found to be crucial in cell differentiation, growth, proliferation, transformation and apoptosis (Boyle and Schreiber, 2007). They regulate diverse cellular functions such as cell signaling, cell adhesion, actin dynamics and signal transduction in transducing extracellular stimuli into cell responses (Raman, 2012).

In the cell

Cervical cancer among women under the age of 30.
A total of 391 women who were under the age of 30 and had had cervical cancer were followed for 14 or more years. Survival rates of those with cervical cancer among women under the age of 30 were not different from the national survival rates, with the exception that there was a significantly higher survival rate for women with cervical cancer who were not premenopausal (P less than 0.05). These results suggest that younger women with cervical cancer have an acceptable chance of survival. This suggests that women with cervical cancer need not necessarily be treated or followed strictly according to age but may accept the use of age-appropriate regimens of treatment.

Q:

A good example of the term "submodular" (which is not the usual concept)

The usual usage of modularity is as follows, with $A$ and $B$ sets.
$Z \subseteq A \subseteq B \implies Z' \subseteq A' \subseteq B'$ (where $Z'=\{A'\cap B^c| A' \in Z$ and if $Z$ is closed under intersection)
I can not find a good real example where the above concept fails, so I wonder if someone can point me to a good example of the term "submodular" meaning the example above. 

A:

The best example I've seen so far is $Z_n$ for $A_{n-1}\times B_n$ with $n\geq 2$, where $A_n$ is the set that consists of those vectors in $\mathbb{R}^n$ that have a non-zero component in the first $n$ coordinates.

Hepatitis C virus (hereinafter referred to as “HCV”) is an enveloped positive RNA-virus belonging to the hepacivirus (group II) and hepatovirus (Group I) family of the retrovirology (Lok et al. JAMA. 281(16): 1633-1642, 1998). It causes an acute hepatitis of unknown mode of occurrence and an insidious disease that spreads to the blood and other body fluids. The virus infects approximately 3% of the global population in a variety of countries. Although it is known that the virus is responsible for cirrhosis of the liver, the exact mechanism of this disease is not completely understood (Klumpp, Curr. Op. Microbiol, May 4, 1999).
HCV is a causative agent of a chronic hepatitis with a higher mortality rate in developing and underdeveloped countries as well as of a liver cancer in the developed world. The infection is usually acute and often accompanied by a jaundice. The infection can also lead to acute disease in the immune competent host.
HCV has not been fully characterized. Although most of the studies focus on the development of antibodies targeting an epitope in the capsid protein

A novel method for the detection of the presence and concentration of a single target nucleic acid sequence in a sample of complex nucleic acid mixtures, such as a mixture of human genomic DNA, has been invented. In one embodiment, the target nucleic acid sequence comprises a target sequence which is associated with a control nucleic acid sequence. If sufficient target sequence is present in the sample, the control sequence will hybridize with the target sequence, thereby producing a characteristic signal. Preferably, a sample comprising a mixture of nucleic acid sequences in both strands hybridizes to the control sequence, thereby producing a characteristic signal. This method is useful for detecting rare copies of target sequences in complex DNA mixtures, and for quantitative detection and quantification of target sequences in complex DNA mixtures.
In a preferred embodiment, a nucleic acid sequence is hybridized to the control sequence comprising DNA having a sequence similar to the target sequence, followed by extension of the control sequence by enzymatic cleavage. In effect the control sequence acts as a "bait" for the target sequence and the hybridization results in the control sequence being "pulled" out of the target DNA. The control sequence can be treated with alkaline phosphatase or restriction endonucleases

Pretreatment in patients with advanced colorectal cancer.
Pretreatment with high dose radiotherapy before chemotherapy can improve 5 year survival rates for patients with locally advanced and metastatic cancers. The benefits appear to be greater when combined with hyperfractionated radiotherapy, and are particularly enhanced by combining these approaches with chemotherapy. Hyperfractionated treatment with radiotherapy and chemotherapy was compared with high-dose treatment with radiotherapy alone in patients with advanced colorectal cancer and resulted in higher disease-free survival (P<0.03), and improved time-to-progression (P=NS). Hyperfractionated radiotherapy is associated with more treatment-related toxicity, particularly with respect to myelosuppression, nausea and vomiting, and mucositis. These findings may have an impact on clinical management of patients with colorectal cancer.

The effects of temperature and salinity on the abundance and composition of bacteria growing in the Arctic coastal seawater.
The Arctic climate has warmed during the last few decades. Increasing temperature and decreasing salinity are the two primary factors affecting the Arctic microbial community, although the effects of temperature and salinity alone on the Arctic bacterial community are still insufficiently understood. We determined the effects of temperature (5-20 °C and 20-35 °C), salinity (5-20 ppt) and the interaction between these two factors on the abundance and structure of the bacterioplankton community. Both the abundance and community structural change significantly with temperature and salinity within the temperature range of 5-35 °C. However, the salinity effects were found to be relatively low. At temperatures of 15 °C and above, the bacterioplankton community structure and abundance exhibited significant variation with salinity, but this change is more significant at lower salinity. The results may suggest that these factors may affect the bacterial community composition and community structures by altering the abundance of certain species, and thereby influencing the bacterioplankton community. These results provide essential information for understanding the bacterioplankton community distribution and abundance in Arctic coastal seawater.

Bacterial biofilms are involved, among others, in a wide variety of human diseases, including respiratory disease, wound infections and other biofilm associated infection diseases. While the mechanisms of biofilm formation are poorly understood, it is believed that biofilm formation requires a complex interplay of microbial physiological state, interactions with surfaces, and interactions with other cell types. For example, a number of clinically relevant bacteria, including Pseudomonas aeruginosa, are known to secrete compounds that may facilitate the formation of aggregated cell masses or biofilms. While the formation of bacterial biofilms is highly dependent on surface interaction, and both physical surfaces (e.g., plastic, metal) and chemical surfaces (e.g., cationic substrates, polystyrene, etc.) are used by many bacteria in this regard as well as some viruses, the effects of bacterial cell density and attachment on biofilm formation are not well understood.
The formation of bacterial biofilms and aggregation is highly dependent on cell-cell signalling and on the interaction of cells with a surface. In particular, cell-cell signalling, which mediates the ability of individual cells to perceive their environment and initiate appropriate responses, is critical to biofilm development. The effect of cyclooxygenase inhibitor and nonsteroidal antiinflammatory drugs on the expression of adhesion molecules on human synovial fibroblasts.
To investigate the effect of cyclooxygenase (COX)-1 and -2 and nonsteroidal antiinflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) on the expression of adhesion molecules on cultured human synovial fibroblasts. Primary cultures of human synovial fibroblast were obtained from knee joints of patients undergoing total knee arthroplasty surgery and were treated with indomethacin, celecoxib or NS-398. Cytomorphologic, immunophenotypic and functional studies were performed. COX-1 and -2 as well as nonsteroidal antiinflammatory drugs have a significant effect on the expression of adhesion molecules in human synovial fibroblast. These effects of NSAIDs are associated with changes in the expression of adhesion molecules and may contribute to the reduction of synovitis and inflammatory joint effusions after prostatic surgery by suppressing the prostaglandin E2 mediated inflammation.

Anatomy, embryology and clinical implications of three-dimensional (3D) surgical planning with stereolithography by computer-aided design and manufacturing procedures.
The use of stereolithography (SL) technology in 3D surgical planning and its role in the development of new surgical procedures and surgical techniques have been the subject of recent investigations and debate. This article analyzes and summarizes current data with the ultimate objective of improving the use of this information both in research and clinical practice. In this review 3D computer modeling systems of the breast are discussed along with their associated surgical implications, surgical technical implications, and their use in the postoperative follow-up of breast operations. The article also presents the current body of research concerning the use of SL for the planning of breast cancer surgery.

A particular embodiment may use various forms of machine-readable media, such as a storage medium.
Machine-readable media, also known as storable media, are physical media that may be used to record machine-readable data for later retrieval and use. This type of media can be any type, shape or form, but examples of machine-readable media include optical disks, magnet-optical disks, magneto-optical disks, magneto-magnetic disks, and solid-state disks. Storable media may be removable or non-removable. For example, removable storable media can be replaced or removed from storage once they have been used up, but non-removable storable media, at least in some forms, can't be replaced or removed from storage once they have been created.
Machine-readable disks and/or records often include one or more data fields, such as an error-correction code or other type of coding applied to data being stored and/or recorded.

Sensors and control systems based on the measurement of magnetic field have become important components in many fields of technology. Such sensors may be used, for example, for position control in mechanical systems, position control in high precision optical measuring equipment and in magnetic resonance imaging. The information that can be obtained from a magnetic field sensor may be summarized as follows: a magnitude or a vector of the magnetic field (an amplitude), a direction of the magnetic field. The sensitivity of the sensor of a magnetic field or a magnetometer depends on the distance of the sensors to the magnetic field. The distance may be expressed as a function of time or the distance may be given in an absolute coordinate system, for example, by a distance to the North Pole of the magnet or by a distance to a magnetized object. A magnetic field sensor may be used to detect the distance. For example, an optical distance meter is based on the principle of the change of the light path of an outgoing light beam that propagates from opposite polarization directions. Other known sensors such as magnetic field sensors based on the Hall effect or magnetic field sensors based on the eddy current effect have also been suggested in order to obtain a distance measurement.

image = pygame.image.load("myimage.png").convert()

How to extract the last line/part of a file in bash

I have a number of files with a fixed length, as below:

file3.txt:
abracadabra
abracadabra
abracadabra

file4.txt:
a
b
c

How can I efficiently split each file in 4 parts using bash?
expected output:
 abracadabra
abracadabra
abracadabra

A:

for f in `cat file*.txt`; do
    part 1: echo "$f" | tr -s " abracadabra" ' '
    part 2: echo "$f" | tr -s "\nabracadabra" ' '
    part 2: echo "$f" | tr -s "\nabracadabra" ' '
done

Explanation
for f in `cat file*.txt`; do
    set -- $f

You set the file (the loop variable $f) with the standard input, to be read.

Determination of polyunsaturated fatty acids by gas chromatography.
Polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFA) represent less than 0.1% of the total fatty acids produced by plants. Recent research on the biochemical and physiological functions carried out by PUFA led the scientists to develop methods for assessing their composition. Among the different techniques used for this purpose, gas chromatography appears to be the most attractive one, because its sensitivity and specificity allow for a determination of fatty acid compositions from extracts or pure molecules or from a mixture of similar fatty acids. This paper discusses the principles of sample treatment, the composition of the sample, and the analytical conditions used for the analysis of PUFA. The review also considers the advantages that this technique offers both to analytical chemists and food scientists. Finally, important information regarding the application of this technique for plant PUFA analysis is presented.

The relationship between personality and psychological distress in the context of cancer therapy: a review.
Personality development is a key factor in the psychological distress of cancer patients. Personality disorders may constitute the best known predisposing and modulating factors underlying the development and maintenance of psychological distress in cancer patients. This review describes the association between personality development in the framework of psychobiological systems and psychological distress in cancer patients treated with chemotherapy and radiotherapy. Psychological distress is influenced by several factors, the most important in its expression being the degree of psychological trauma experienced by patients and related factors. Personality disorders are highly prevalent among patients with cancer and constitute the best known predisposing and modulating factors of psychological distress in these patients. The current data seem to indicate that personality disorder may be a more potent risk factor for psychological distress than some other risk factors such as sex, somatization, the psychological symptoms experienced by patients at the time of diagnosis of malignancy, psychosocial factors such as coping patterns, personality style, life stressors and the degree of adaptation to cancer treatment.

The present invention pertains to integrated circuits and more particularly to integrated circuit memory cells having a field effect transistor in a stacked capacitor configuration.
One problem which is of particular concern in the design and fabrication of dynamic random access memory (DRAM) cells with thin film field effect transistors is that the cell layout is constrained by the minimum size required by the circuit for the access transistor. As a general rule, as the size of the circuit design decreases, the minimum size of the circuit design, on a circuit board or equivalent packaging substrate, correspondingly decreases. For this reason, it is preferable to minimize the surface area of a DRAM memory cell since this is more likely to be used in the case of a semiconductor integrated circuit of a given size than would be a large array of memory cells. The minimum circuit design size is limited for a variety of reasons, including fabrication and package limitations, etc. As to fabrication limitations, it should be noted that as the surface area of a particular integrated circuit grows larger in proportion to the surface area of the circuit, the minimum design size is also correspondingly larger. In addition, research and development on an organic light emitting device (hereinafter, OLED) in which a light emitting material is interposed between anode and cathode has been actively performed in order to obtain a portable thin device such as an electronic wristwatch, a light source for illumination and the like. Among these, an organic light emitting device with a structure of sequentially stacking anode, hole transporting layer, light emitting layer, and electron transporting layer on a substrate has been researched and developed for the purpose of improving the light emitting efficiency.
However, in such an organic light emitting device, a process of forming a light emitting layer from a light emitting material is problematic in that the light emitting efficiency is not excellent, although the organic light emitting layer has high light emitting efficiency.

[Treatment of osteochondral defects in the knee using a vascularised bone block: A case report].
To investigate the effect of using a bone graft combined with vascularised fibular bone graft for treatment of osteonecrosis in the knee and to review the literature concerned. The clinical data of a 35-years-old patient with osteonecrosis was collected, and the diagnosis confirmed by magnetic resonance imaging, and the method, including the reconstruction of the articular surface, the defect filling, and the postoperative radiotherapy therapy, were discussed in detail. According to the patient's request, autologous bone graft material and bone fragment were collected; after the blood supply of the vascularised fibula was reestablished, the transplantation surgery of the autologous bone graft and the filling of the defect were performed simultaneously. The patient showed no complications at the follow-up, and the symptoms were completely disappeared. The vascularised fibular bone graft has advantages of having no donor site and being easy to transplant. It also has relatively good bone regeneration. This method is worthy of further exploration. .

Bovine viral diarrhea virus-4 (BVDV-4) vaccine and natural hosts of BVDV-4 isolated from bovine milk.
Bovine viral diarrhea virus (BVDV), also known as bovine viral diarrhea virus-1 (BVDV-1), bovine viral diarrhea virus-2 (BVDV-2), and bovine viral diarrhea virus-3 (BVDV-3), is the most prevalent and economically important member of the family Pestiviridae, which causes bovine respiratory disease (BRD). Currently available inactivated and recombinant oral vaccines against BVDV-1 and BVDV-2 are not able to cross-protect against BVDV-3. Since bovine respiratory disease is a severe disease in the cattle industry causing severe economic losses to the cattle industry, further development of BVDV protection against BVDV-3 is desperately needed. In this study we examined the ability of BVDV-4, the only currently available subtype of the BVDV-3, to be a live vaccine against BVDV-3. Five different genotypes of BVDV-4 were isolated from milk of a dairy cow.

The present invention relates to the field of computer security and, more particularly, to mechanisms for protecting computer files and programs on computers against unauthorized editing and copying in order to preserve data integrity.
A variety of schemes exist for protecting computers against unauthorized editing or copying of computer programs or other types of data (hereinafter referred to as files). One example of such a scheme is to limit the maximum number of times such a program or other file can be copied or altered by a given user. Although limited user copy and editing control is a security measure that is desirable from a theoretical standpoint, it often proves uneconomically restrictive on a practical basis. For example, software developers often provide a program or other file for a particular client which cannot be further distributed unless the program or file is given a pre-determined level of protection, usually one or more of the following: restrictive time limitations, file age limits, software level security, or restricted network access to the program or file, or otherwise limiting access to, or making it unavailable to the target end user.

(p, q, A) {\left| q,\beta\right>>}$ where $A$ is an operator (generate the Hilbert space). The operator $A$ is a linear combination of creation and annihilation operators. Since we need to take states with odd particle numbers, $q$ must be odd, e.g. $(p^4_1, p^4_2, q^6_1)$. Therefore for the ground state $q$ must have the same parity, i.e. $(p^4, p^4, q^6)$. This in turn implies that the parity of $Q$ will be equal to the parity of $A$ and also that $\beta$ in the state will have the same parity. Therefore we see that any ground state is $$\lvert A\rangle = \prod_{q^6} C_q (A)$$ and that this is equivalent to the original Fock space.

G-Tec is a brand of electric multiple unit high-speed rail cars designed by GamaTEC and manufactured by General Electric Company, based in Fairfield, Connecticut.  These intercity electric rail cars operate in electrified third rail routes in the Northeastern United States on the Connecticut, New England, New York and Canadian border as well as the New Jersey and New York metropolitan area, where the electrification allows these trains to operate at 400 km/h (250 mph). [A case of aortic root aneurysm complicated with a mediastinal hematoma].
We report a 71-year-old man with aortic root aneurysm. The diagnosis was made at another hospital when the patient complained of breathlessness. An angiocardiogram at that time showed dilatation of the ascending aorta, aortic root aneurysm at the level of sinotubular junction and rupture of the left upper and middle pulmonary artery. The aneurysm diameter, which had decreased to 25 mm after surgery, was larger than that observed at the previous hospital. Cardiac catheterization revealed a patent coronary artery, normal coronary flow and an aorto-ventricular gradient of 30 mmHg. A mediastinal hematoma was also detected in chest X-ray. The patient was transferred to our hospital, and the aneurysm was repaired using a Dacron tube graft. Cardiac catheterization at the beginning revealed good cardiac function, suggesting that the patient had no cardiac dysfunction from his mediastinal hematoma. The aneurysm diameter decreased from 30 mm to 17 mm and that of the left pulmonary artery showed regression from 30 mm

1. Field of the Invention
The present invention relates to a data storage device such as a disc device, and more particularly to a technique to eliminate the possibility of wrong reading performed during readout of data from a disc device.
2. Description of Related Art
As data-processing devices of the kind comprising a disc device, a magnetic disc device or an optical disc device for a recording/reproducing apparatus, etc., are becoming more sophisticated, it becomes desirable to have the ability to execute an error correction code (ECC: a code used to detect an error) at the time of writing or reading from the disc device. The ability to perform ECC makes it possible to reduce the number of data errors which are capable of being detected, without the use of a parity bit, by the error correction code.
The error correction code may also help to prevent a disc device from being degraded by repeated access or may help to recover from a degraded state by recovering from a data error or disc error.

With the advances in signal processing capabilities, audio signals have been improved beyond the capabilities of the human speech signals with ever greater fidelity and clarity. However, the audio quality output is limited by the relatively low level and/or distortion of the output signal. The fundamental problem is that as the signal level increases, the signal is saturated and distortion is introduced, thereby limiting the quality of the output signal. For example, as the level increases, the level beyond the saturation point will be non-linear distortion.
In general, the human hearing system is able to process input signals with a higher frequency range than their output signal and the human ear is able to distinguish lower frequency sounds from higher frequencies without any noticeable distortion at the frequency of the lower frequency sound.
